language.name: Dromite
entries: Sarlona's civilizations aren't just found on the surface. Deep below the surface, tunnels connect the city-hives of the dromites, a short, insectoid people with innate psionic powers. Their origins are a mystery to the surface dwellers; dromites notably do not tell their people's history to outsiders, though most assume that they emerged from Khyber. Regardless of the truth, dromites care more about the future than the past. Time already passed is set in stone and can only be carved, never altered. Time yet to be flows like water, ever-changing. Dromites age at about the same rate as humans and can live up to 140 years old.

Raised in communal clutches, dromites choose their role in society as part of their transition into adulthood. While called 'castes' by outsiders, dromites describe it as an 'essence form', as it informs more than just one's place in society. An essence form is something akin to a gender identity, job title, personality descriptor, physical resistance, and psionic energy manifestation rolled into one. It is ultimately a choice, one that changes a dromite's physical and mental capabilities. Dromites undergo these changes willingly, and choosing one's essence form is cause for great celebration.

Dromites recognize four primary essence forms. Torch dromites are passionate guards and soldiers, able to manifest arcs of fire through manipulating psionic energy. Ice dromites are methodical lorekeepers, turning this careful mental state into blasts of ice. Voice dromites love performance and artistry, using their voices and sound to emit bursts of thunder. Glimmer dromites are ambitious and energetic artisans, turning their own energy into crackling lightning.

Each dromite city-hive is ruled by at least one Grand Queen and several Elected Consorts. Each is chosen through democratic elections, and either position can be held by a member of any essence form. Once elected, the city hive pools the collective faith of their people and large amounts of psionic power into the elected dromites, greatly bolstering their psionic abilities. Queen and consorts use this power to create the next generation of dromites within the city hive, devoting most of their time to creating eggs, as dromites have difficulty producing children outside of this process. Even so, their words and proclamations carry weight, and the ruling bodies strive to maintain peace and cohesion among their people.

City-hives can be found all throughout Sarlona. Many are located under the once-great cities of Old Sarlona, and as a result, have vast resources and knowledge that was otherwise lost in the Sundering. Despite their relative isolation, all hives maintain good relations and will unite against a larger threat, mobilizing through their system of subterranean tunnels. This highly advanced tunnel system also includes shortcuts through Khyber demiplanes; highly skilled guides can get from one end of Sarlona to another in under a day by passing through parts of Khyber.

The dromite tunnel network is a double-edged sword. Riedra desperately wants access to the network to invade Adar, and dromites are routinely captured and enslaved when the Unity is able to locate a city hive. As a result, surface entrances are a closely guarded secret, though dromites are more than happy to aid Riedra's rebel groups when possible.

Their proximity to Khyber also makes for a risky living situation—especially when the daelkyr Valaara takes a particular interest in city-hives. Those who follow the Crawling Queen and embrace its gifts change their essence form entirely, swapping elemental damage for psychic assaults on the mind. This so-called "Swarm Essence" preaches unifying their people into a greater hivemind, one that will someday breach the surface world and claim it for all dromite-kind. Dromites consider the Crawling Queen a dangerous influence and will root out signs of daelkyr corruption with extreme prejudice.

subclassFeature.name: Way of the Dreaming Fist
entries: Though the Inspired are capable of devastating psionic power in Riedra, the quori are limited in number, and they can only possess one empty vessel at a time. When skirmishes happen and combat must be had, Riedra instead turns to Chosen unarmed fighters wielding the powers of Dal Quor. In response, the kalashtar developed similar techniques drawing upon their quori, dubbing their warriors the 'fists of Dal Quor'.

subclassFeature.name: Enlightenment Reveals the Dream
entries: You can use your ki to duplicate the effects of certain spells. As an action, you can spend 2 ki points to cast {@spell detect thoughts|PHB}, {@spell dream link|TalvakrisGuidetoAdar}, {@spell invisibility|PHB}, or {@spell see invisibility|PHB}, without requiring material components. Additionally, you gain the {@spell message|PHB} cantrip if you don't already know it.

subclassFeature.name: Vision Conquers the Adversary
entries: You can choose for your unarmed strikes to deal psychic damage instead of bludgeoning damage. Additionally, whenever you spend at least one ki point on your turn, your unarmed strikes deal extra psychic damage equal to half your Proficiency Bonus (round down) until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Will Disproves the Truth
entries: You learned that time and space are but illusions to the determined dreamer. You may spend 4 ki points as a bonus action to cast {@spell dimension door}, requiring no components.

Alternatively, once per turn, when you hit a creature with an unarmed strike, you may spend 4 ki points to forgo dealing damage with the attack to instead cast {@spell banishment} on the target of the attack, requiring no components.

subclassFeature.name: Tranquility Wakes the Dreamer
entries: Whenever you hit a creature with an unarmed strike, you may spend 8 ki points to forgo dealing damage with the attack and instead cast {@spell feeblemind} on the attack's target, requiring no action or spell components. You can use this feature once, and you must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

subclassFeature.name: Way of the Elemental Master
entries: Ghoza Monastery was built within a great Kythri manifest zone. Within the shifting elemental halls, its martial artists have developed a fighting style that draws upon the Churning Chaos, blending them together in a seamless display of elemental power. Practitioners can summon jets of flame, bend water to their will, and at  their pinnacle, even control the weather.

entries.subclassFeature: Assault of the Land, Sea, and Sky|Monk|PHB|Elemental Master|TalvakrisGuidetoAdar|3

subclassFeature.name: Hands Shape the World
entries: Using magically potent hand and arm gestures, you can control the elements. You learn the {@spell control flames|PHB}, {@spell create bonfire|PHB}, {@spell gust|PHB}, {@spell mold earth|PHB}, and {@spell shape water|PHB} cantrips if you don't already know them. You can cast {@spell create bonfire|PHB} and {@spell gust|PHB} using only somatic components.

subclassFeature.name: Assault of the Land, Sea, and Sky
entries: When you strike your foes, you can summon the fires and tremors of the earth, the thunder of the clouds, and the lashing waves of the sea. Immediately after you take the {@action Attack} action on your turn, you can spend 1 ki point to use one of the following magical effects. You can choose a new effect each time you use this feature.

entries.name: Earthen Tremor
entries: You strike the ground and cause a disruptive tremor to emanate in a 5-foot radius from a point you can see on the ground within 15 feet of you.

Each creature in contact with the ground within the area must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes bludgeoning damage equal to {@dice 1d4} + your Wisdom modifier and is knocked {@condition prone}. If the ground in that area is loose earth or stone, it becomes {@quickref difficult terrain||3} until cleared (requiring at least 1 minute of manual labor to do so by hand). This effect's damage dice increases when you reach 5th level ({@dice 2d4}), 11th level ({@dice 3d4}), and 17th level ({@dice 4d4}).

entries.name: Flame Bolt
entries: Make a ranged spell attack against a creature or object within 120 feet of you. On a hit, the target takes fire damage equal to {@dice 1d10} + your Wisdom modifier. A flammable object hit by this effect ignites if it isn't being worn or carried. This effect's damage dice increases when you reach 5th level ({@dice 2d10}), 11th level ({@dice 3d10}), and 17th level ({@dice 4d10}).

entries.name: Thunder Impact
entries: Make a melee spell attack against a creature within 30 feet of you. On a hit, the target takes thunder damage equal to {@dice 1d8} + your Wisdom modifier and you can force the creature to make a Constitution saving throw, pushing it 10 feet directly away from you and knocking it {@condition prone} on a failed saving throw. This effect's damage dice increases when you reach 5th level ({@dice 2d8}), 11th level ({@dice 3d8}), and 17th level ({@dice 4d8}).

entries.name: Water Whip
entries: Choose a source of water that you can see within 60 feet of you that has a volume of at least 1 liter. Make a melee spell attack against a creature within 30 feet of the source of water. On a hit, the target takes slashing damage equal to {@dice 1d6} + your Wisdom modifier and you can force the creature to make a Strength saving throw, pulling it up to 10 feet towards the body of water on a failed saving throw. This effect's damage dice increases when you reach 5th level ({@dice 2d6}), 11th level ({@dice 3d6}), and 17th level ({@dice 4d6}).

subclassFeature.name: Body of Cloud and Wave
entries: You can use your ki to duplicate the effects of certain spells. As an action, you can spend 3 ki points to cast {@spell gaseous form}, {@spell fly|PHB}, {@spell water breathing|PHB}, or {@spell water walk|PHB}, requiring only somatic components.

subclassFeature.name: Breath of Fire
entries: As an action, you can spend 4 ki points to exhale a blast of fiery breath in a 60-foot cone. Each creature in the cone must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 5d10} fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. You can use this feature once, and you must finish a short or long rest before you can do so again.

As you grow stronger, you can spend additional ki points when you use this feature to increase its damage. Each additional ki point you spend increases the feature's damage by {@dice 1d10}. The maximum number of ki points (5 plus any additional points) that you can spend on the feature equals half your monk level.

subclassFeature.name: Eternal Mountain Defense
entries: You can magically alter your form to be as enduring as stone. You may spend 4 ki points as an action to cast {@spell stoneskin}, requiring no components.

subclassFeature.name: Master of Earth, Sea, and Sky
entries: You can use your ki to duplicate the effects of certain spells. You can spend 8 ki points to cast {@spell control weather}, {@spell earthquake}, or {@spell tsunami}, requiring only somatic components. You can use this feature once, and you must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

subclassFeature.name: Way of the Summit Sentinel
entries: While Hazgaal is best known for his acceptance of the rebel quori to Adar, before he became Haztaratai, he developed a form of martial arts that merged Adar's sturdy mountains with the crackling lightning of its storms. The Haztaratain order is its official name, but it is better known colloquially as the Way of the Summit Sentinel (or parahdi in Adaran).

subclassFeature.name: Mountain Acclimation
entries: You are naturally acclimated to high altitudes. This includes elevations above 20,000 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Sentinel Stances
entries: You can focus your ki in emulation of the mountains to enter a magical stance. Once per turn, requiring no action, you may spend 1 ki to enter or change your stance to one of the ones listed below. You gain the stance's effects until you enter a different stance, become {@condition incapacitated}, or finish a short rest.

entries.name: Cliff Stride
entries: You gain a climb speed equal to your walking speed, and can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

entries.name: Pinnacle Endurance
entries: You gain resistance to cold and lightning damage.

entries.name: Stone Serenity
entries: You cannot be knocked {@condition prone}. Additionally, you cannot be moved from your space by other creatures against your will, except by effects that teleport you or transport you to another plane.

subclassFeature.name: Storm and Stone Technique
entries: You can use your ki to briefly wreathe your fists in lightning. When you hit a creature with an unarmed strike, you can spend 1 ki point to deal extra lightning damage equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die. You can use this feature only once per attack.

subclassFeature.name: Sustenance of the Precipice
entries: You become able to sustain yourself on breath alone. You no longer require food or drink.

subclassFeature.name: The Mountain Does Not Fall
entries: You can draw on the strength of mountains to endure any pain. On your turn, you can spend 5 ki points, requiring no action, to begin {@status concentration||concentrating} (as though {@status concentration||concentrating} on a spell) for up to 1 minute. While you maintain your {@status concentration}, your hit points cannot drop below 1 while you are in contact with the ground. If your {@status concentration} is broken due to taking damage while you are in contact with the ground, your {@status concentration} ends but your hit points are not reduced below 1 by that damage. You can use this feature once, and you must finish a short or long rest before you can do so again.

subclassFeature.name: Peak of Calm, Zenith of Wrath
entries: You can use your ki to duplicate the effects of certain spells. You can spend 8 ki points to cast {@spell control weather} or 9 ki points to cast {@spell storm of vengeance}. You can use this feature once, and you must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

subclassFeature.name: Quori Mindhunter
entries: Those who learn the truth of the Dreaming Dark are often terrified by its implications for Eberron and the world. A small number of those choose to strike back against the quori. An even smaller number choose to fight the quori with their own. The quori mindhunters have found ways to trap quori within their minds, drawing upon the nightmare spirit and its power while keeping it bound. It is one of the most dangerous ways to fight the Dreaming Dark, as the bound quori is constantly struggling against its bonds, but the powers it can bring against the Inspired are devastating.

subclassFeature.name: Eschew Dreaming
entries: You become immune to possession and you cannot be magically put to sleep unless you wish to be. Additionally, you no longer require sleep, and you can finish a long rest in 4 hours, provided you spend those hours in meditation. You remain conscious and aware of your surroundings while meditating this way.

subclassFeature.name: Mindhunter Magic
entries: You learn an additional spell when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown in the Mindhunter Spells table. The spell counts as a ranger spell for you, but it doesn't count against the number of ranger spells you know.

9th
{@spell magic circle|PHB}
13th

subclassFeature.name: Nightmare Strikes
entries: Drawing on the techniques of those you hunt, you can augment your strikes with power drawn from nightmares. When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can deal extra psychic damage to the target equal to half your Proficiency Bonus (round up).

subclassFeature.name: Psychic Turning
entries: You can enter a battle of wills and attempt to expel spiritual and mental parasites. As an action, you can send out a subtle psychic shockwave only perceptible to possessing creatures. Each creature currently possessing another creature within 30 feet of you must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. A quori must make this saving throw with disadvantage. On a failed save, the creature is turned for 1 minute or until it takes damage.

A creature turned by this effect must use its reaction to immediately leave the creature it has possessed, or as a bonus action on its next turn if it has already used its reaction, appearing within reach of its former host. A creature that would normally return to a different plane of existence when expelled from its host instead manifests on your plane of existence, and is not returned to its plane when this effect's duration is over.

For the duration, a turned creature must spend its turns trying to move as far away from you as it can, and it can't willingly move to a space within 30 feet of you. It also can't take reactions after leaving its host. For its action, it can use only the {@action Dash} action or try to escape from an effect that prevents it from moving. If there's nowhere to move, the creature can use the {@action Dodge} action. If the creature's true form is concealed by an illusion, shapeshifting, or other effect, that form is revealed while it is turned.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Hostility Backlash
entries: When you take damage from a creature you can see within 60 feet of you, as a reaction you can force that creature to make an Intelligence saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 2d10} force damage.
The damage increases to {@dice 3d10} when you reach 17th level in this class.

subclassFeature.name: Guarded Mind
entries: When you would fail an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma saving throw, you can reroll the saving throw with advantage. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Manifest Power
entries: TBD

subclassFeature.name: Manifest Magic
entries: You learn additional spells as shown in the Manifest Magic table. These spells don't count against your spells known.

{@spell detect evil and good|PHB}, {@spell detect magic|PHB}, {@spell protection from evil and good|PHB}
3rd

subclassFeature.name: Detect Planar Breach
entries: You can sense the direction and distance to the nearest planar breach.
Usable a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus per long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Planar Boon
entries: After a long rest, you attune to a plane and gain a special benefit until your next rest.

subclassFeature.name: Pocket Space
entries: You gain access to a personal extradimensional space and can store or retrieve objects using bonus actions or sorcery points.

subclassFeature.name: Manifested Self
entries: You can transform into summoned creatures by expending sorcery points, replacing your stats temporarily.

subclassFeature.name: Erudite
entries: You gain proficiency in the Arcana skill.
You can use Intelligence as your spellcasting ability instead of Charisma.

rows: 1st
{@spell cause fear|PHB}, {@spell dissonant whispers|PHB}
