While cursed, the creature has disadvantage on all attack rolls against creatures other than Shan Varatel. The curse ends if Shan Varatel uses this action against another creature, or if Shan Varatel can't see the cursed target for 1 week.

reaction.name: Was That Your Best Shot?
entries: When Shan Varatel would take damage, they don't.

legendary.name: Bounding Stride
entries: Shan Varatel moves up to their speed without provoking opportunity attacks. This movement isn't slowed if they are currently grappling a creature.

legendary.name: Fey Fists of Fury (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Shan Varatel makes two Slam attacks.

legendary.name: The Champion's Decree (Costs 3 Actions)
entries: One creature Shan Varatel is grappling must make a Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check contested by Shan Varatel's Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check. If the result of Shan Varatel's check is higher than the {@condition grappled} creature's, Shan Varatel leaps up top 3 miles straight up with the {@condition grappled} creature, then instantly lands with the creature splayed across their knee, instantly reducing the creature's hit points to 0.

resist.note: damage from nonmagical attacks made with weapons that aren't silvered

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Taavali casts one of the following spells, requiring no somatic or material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 18}):

trait.name: Discern Honesty
entries: Taavali knows if he hears a lie.

trait.name: Inscrutable
entries: Taavali is immune to any effect that would sense his emotions or read his thoughts, as well as any divination spell that he refuses. Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) checks made to ascertain Taavali's intentions or sincerity have disadvantage.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (2/Day)
entries: If Taavali fails a saving throw, they can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: Taavali has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Regeneration
entries: Taavali regains 30 hit points at the start of his turn if he has at least 1 hit point and is in contact with the waters of Dvaarnava Harbor.

trait.name: Rejuvenation
entries: If Taavali dies, he returns to life in {@dice 1d6} days, appearing anywhere in the waters of Dvaarnava Harbor and regaining all his hit points. Taavali's return can only be prevented with a carefully worded {@spell wish} spell or if the entire Dvaarnava Harbor has dried up.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Taavali makes one Bite attack and two Constrict attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) piercing damage.

action.name: Constrict
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 1d10 + 5}) bludgeoning damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 20}). Until this grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained}. Taavali's sinuous body is large enough to grapple one Huge target, two Large targets, or four Medium or smaller targets. Taavali can't constrict another target if he has reached his maximum number of grapple targets.

action.name: Breath Weapon {@recharge 5}
entries: Taavali exhales a blast of chilling breath in a 150-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes 21 ({@damage 6d6}) cold damage and its speed is reduced to 0 until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage and its speed isn't reduced.

legendary.name: Snapping Jaws
entries: Taavali makes a Bite attack.

legendary.name: Surging Coils (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Taavali swims up to his swim speed.
This movement isn't slowed if he is currently grappling one or more creatures.

legendary.name: Chilling Breath (Costs 3 Actions)
entries: Taavali uses his Breath Weapon if he can, or automatically recharges it if he can't.

trait.name: Air Form (Air Only)
entries: Ahdryatmin can enter a hostile creature's space and stop there. They can move through any space that isn't airtight without squeezing.

trait.name: All-Around Vision
entries: Ahdryatmin can't be {@status surprised} while they aren't {@condition incapacitated}.

trait.name: Earth Glide (Earth Only)
entries: Ahdryatmin can burrow through unworked earth and stone. While doing so, they don't disturb the material they move through.

trait.name: Flyby (Air Only)
entries: Ahdryatmin doesn't provoke opportunity attacks when they fly out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Magic Attacks
entries: Ahdryatmin's weapon attacks are magical.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (5/Day)
entries: If Ahdryatmin fails a saving throw, they can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Siege Lord (Earth Only)
entries: Ahdryatmin deals ten times damage to objects and structures.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Ahdryatmin makes three Fist of the Mountain or Slash of the Sky attacks, appropriate to their elemental form.

action.name: Fist of the Mountain (Earth Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 16} to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. {@h}40 ({@damage 6d10 + 7}) bludgeoning damage plus 11 ({@damage 2d10}) force damage. If Ahdryatmin hits one Huge or smaller creature with this attack at least twice in a turn, the creature must make a {@dc 25} Strength saving throw. On a failure, the creature is knocked {@condition prone} and is buried under 5 feet of rubble. A buried creature can't breathe until it is freed, which can be accomplished if it or an ally uses an action to make a {@dc 25} Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check, excavating the creature on a success.

action.name: Slash of the Sky (Air Only)
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 16} to hit, reach 20 ft. or range 500 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 2d10 + 7}) slashing damage plus 33 ({@damage 6d10}) force damage. If Adhyratmin hits one Large or smaller creature with this attack at least twice in a turn, the creature must make a {@dc 25} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, the creature is knocked {@condition prone} and blown away, flung up to 60 feet away from Ahdryatmin. If a flung creature collides with an immovable object (such as the ground or an ancient tree) or another creature, the flung creature takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) bludgeoning damage for every 10 feet it was thrown before impact. If the flung creature collides with another creature, that other creature must succeed on a {@dc 23} Dexterity saving throw or take the same impact damage and be knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Fling (Earth Only)
entries: Ahdryatmin hauls up a chunk of rubble, debris, or the ground that is a cube 10 feet on a side and throws it at a point they can see within 1 mile. Each creature in the path of the thrown chunk or within a 30-foot radius of where it lands must make a {@dc 24} Dexterity saving throw or take 71 ({@damage 11d12}) bludgeoning damage on a failed save, or half as much on a success. The area of impact then becomes difficult terrain.

legendary.name: Elemental Movement
entries: Ahdryatmin burrows (Earth Only) or flies (Air Only) up to their speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

legendary.name: Nailed to the Sky (Air Only; Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Ahdryatmin targets one creature they can see, which must succeed on a {@dc 27} Strength saving throw, or be instantly launched into the sky far, far above

legendary.name: Adar
entries: At its new altitude, the creature cannot breathe and it takes 1 cold damage and 1 radiant damage at the start of each minute it remains there. A creature that can't fly is stuck at this altitude until it begins to fall back to Adar one hour later. A flying creature can begin a controlled freefall back to the ground immediately, returning to the area from which it was launched in a minute if it has a flying speed less than 60 feet, or at the end of its next turn if it has a flying speed of 60 feet or faster.

legendary.name: Shackled to the Earth (Earth Only; Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Each creature of Adhryatmin's choice that they can see must make a {@dc 27} Charisma saving throw. On a failure, a creature is cursed. While cursed this way, a creature must always be touching solid ground, and is unable to jump, fly, or take the Dash action. If a creature is flying when it fails the saving throw, it instantly plummets straight down to the ground with supernatural speed, taking twice the normal amount of falling damage for the distance it fell. The curse remains until it is removed by a {@spell remove curse} spell, a {@spell greater restoration} spell, or similar magic.

legendary.name: Primal Onslaught (Costs 3 Actions)
entries: Ahdryatmin uses Multiattack.

trait.name: Aura of Nightfire
entries: At the start of each of Vhaga Anulash's turns, each creature within 5 feet of them takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) necrotic damage. A creature that touches Vhaga Anulash or hits them with a melee attack while within 5 feet of them takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) necrotic damage.

trait.name: Implosive Demise
entries: When Vhaga Anulash dies, they collapse into a momentary black hole. Each creature within 30 feet of them must make a {@dc 21} Constitution saving throw, taking 70 ({@damage 20d6}) force damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

Any creature within 10 feet of Vhaga Anulash when they implode automatically fails this saving throw. If this damage leaves a creature with 0 hit points, the creature and everything it is wearing and carrying, except magic items, are completely and utterly annihilated, leaving behind not even dust. The target can be restored to life only by means of a true resurrection or a {@spell wish} spell.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If Vhaga Anulash fails a saving throw, they can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Limited Magic Immunity
entries: Vhaga Anulash can't be affected or detected by spells of 6th-level or lower unless they wish to be. They have advantage on saving throws against all other spells and magical effects.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: Vhaga Anulash's weapon attacks are magical.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Vhaga Anulash makes two attacks: one with the Annihilating Blade and one with their Whip.

action.name: Annihilating Blade
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 15} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}30 ({@damage 4d10 + 8}) force damage. If the target was a creature and this damage leaves it with 0 hit points or Vhaga Anulash rolled a 20 on the attack roll, the target and everything it is wearing and carrying, except magic items, are completely and utterly annihilated, leaving behind not even dust. The target can be restored to life only by means of a true resurrection or a {@spell wish} spell.

action.name: Whip
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 15} to hit, reach 30 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d6 + 8}) slashing damage plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) necrotic damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 23} Strength saving throw or be pulled up to 25 feet toward Vhaga Anulash.

bonus.name: Teleport
entries: Vhaga Anulash magically teleports, along with any equipment they are wearing or carrying, up to 120 feet to an unoccupied space they can see.

legendary.name: Attack
entries: Vhaga Anulash makes one attack with their whip.

legendary.name: Teleport
entries: Vhaga Anulash uses their {@spell Teleport|PHB} action.

legendary.name: Gravity Flare (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Each creature within 30 feet of Vhaga Anulash must succeed on a {@dc 21} Strength saving throw or be pulled up to 20 feet toward Vhaga Anulash. Then, each creature within 15 feet of Vhaga Anulash takes 20 ({@damage 4d6 + 6}) necrotic damage.

legendary.name: Blade of Demise (Costs 3 Actions)
entries: Vhaga Anulash makes one attack with the Annihilating Blade.

resist.note: except Radiant damage dealt by good creatures

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Ran Iishiv casts one of the following spells, requiring no spell components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 27}):

trait.name: Annihilating Touch
entries: Once per turn, a creature that touches Ran Iishiv or hits it with a melee attack takes 22 ({@damage 4d10}) force damage.

trait.name: Incorporeal Being
entries: Ran Iishiv can move through and occupy the space of other creatures and objects, and its Armor Class uses its Wisdom modifier instead of its Dexterity modifier. Ran Iishiv may also target, attack, and affect creatures on the Ethereal Plane as if they were on the Material Plane. Additionally, all nonmagical attacks against Ran Iishiv automatically miss.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If Ran Iishiv fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: Ran Iishiv has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Siege Lord
entries: Ran Iishiv deals ten times damage to objects and structures.

The End is Nigh. When Ran Iishiv deals force damage to a creature or object that leaves it with 0 hit points, the creature or object must succeed on a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw or it ceases to exist. A creature or object that ceases to exist can only be restored to existence with a carefully worded {@spell wish} spell.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Ran Iishiv makes four Oblivion's Caress attacks.
Alternatively, it casts {@spell disintegrate} twice.

action.name: Oblivion's Caress
entries: {@atk ms} +18, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}39 ({@damage 6d12}) force damage.

reaction.name: Spell Sink
entries: Ran Iishiv targets one creature it can see within 120 feet of it that is casting a spell. The target must succeed on a spellcasting ability check with a DC equal to 10 + the spell's level, or else the spell fails and has no effect.

legendary.name: Brush with Disaster
entries: Ran Iishiv makes an Oblivion's Caress attack.

legendary.name: Magic of Ending (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Ran Iishiv uses its Spellcasting action.

legendary.name: A Glimpse of the End (Costs 3 Actions)
entries: Each creature within 120 feet of Ran Iishiv must make a {@dc 17} Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, a creature is reduced to 0 hit points.

immune.note: except Radiant damage dealt by good creatures

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Ran Iishiv casts one of the following spells, requiring no spell components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 27}):

trait.name: Annihilation Incarnate
entries: A creature that touches Ran Iishiv or hits it with a melee attack takes 22 ({@damage 4d10}) force damage.

trait.name: Incorporeal Being
entries: Ran Iishiv can move through and occupy the space of other creatures and objects, and its Armor Class uses its Wisdom modifier instead of its Dexterity modifier. Ran Iishiv may also target, attack, and affect creatures on the Ethereal Plane as if they were on the Material Plane. Additionally, all nonmagical attacks against Ran Iishiv automatically miss.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (5/Day)
entries: If Ran Iishiv fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Sense Magic
entries: Ran Iishiv can sense the presence of magic within 500 feet of it, seeing a faint aura around any visible creature or object that bears magic, and determining its school.

trait.name: Siege Lord
entries: Ran Iishiv deals ten times damage to objects and structures.

trait.name: Spell Immunity
entries: Ran Iishiv can't be affected or detected by spells unless it wishes to be. It has advantage on saving throws against all other magical effects.

trait.name: The End of All Things
entries: All force damage Ran Iishiv deals ignores resistance or immunity to force damage and cannot otherwise be mitigated in any way. Additionally, when Ran Iishiv deals force damage to a creature or object that leaves it with 0 hit points, the creature or object ceases to exist. The creature or object can only be restored to existence with a carefully worded {@spell wish} spell.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Ran Iishiv makes four Disaster attacks. It can cast {@spell disintegrate} in place of any of these attacks.

action.name: Disaster
entries: {@atk ms} +19, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}78 ({@damage 12d12}) force damage.

bonus.name: Change Shape
entries: Ran Iishiv can assume the form of any creature or being, or a unique shape. The form can be of any size. Its statistics do not change, regardless of form, and it maintains the form indefinitely until a new form is chosen. Truesight reveals that Ran Iishiv's form is an assumed one, but does not reveal its true form, for it doesn't truly have one.

reaction.name: Unravel Casting
entries: Ran Iishiv targets one creature it can see within 500 feet of it that is casting a spell. The target's spell fails and has no effect.

legendary.name: Oblivion
entries: Ran Iishiv makes a Disaster attack or casts {@spell disintegrate}.

legendary.name: End Magic (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Ran Iishiv casts a {@spell dispel magic} on every magical effect it can sense.

legendary.name: Unmake (Costs 3 Actions)
entries: Ran Iishiv casts {@spell power word kill} on every creature of its choice within 500 feet of it.

monster.name: Dream Spirit

ac.special: 16 (natural armor) (Despair, Fear, Fury, or Joy only); 18 (natural armor) (Pride only)

hp.special: 50 (Despair, Fear, Fury, or Joy only); 60 (Pride only)

fly.condition: (hover; Despair, Fury, Joy, or Pride only)

languages: understands the languages you speak; telepathy 60 ft.

trait.name: Aura of Apathy (Despair Only)
entries: When a creature the spirit can see starts its turn within 10 feet of the spirit, the spirit can force it to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the target can't use reactions and its speed is halved until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Spider Climb (Fear Only)
entries: The spirit can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Unassailable Presence (Pride Only)
entries: A creature that touches the spirit or hits it with an attack while within 15 feet of it takes {@damage 1d10} psychic damage.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The spirit makes two attacks. If the spirit is a Despair spirit, it makes a third attack.

action.name: Rend
entries: {@atk mw} {@hitYourSpellAttack your spell attack modifier} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}{@damage 1d6 + 8} slashing damage.

action.name: Mind Strike
entries: {@atk rs} {@hitYourSpellAttack your spell attack modifier} to hit, range 90 ft., one creature. {@h}{@damage 2d10} psychic damage.

action.name: Burning Rage (Fury Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hitYourSpellAttack your spell attack modifier} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}{@damage 1d10 + 8} fire damage, and the spirit gains {@dice 1d10} temporary hit points.

bonus.name: Jump Scare (Fear Only)
entries: The spirit magically teleports up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see. One creature within 10 feet of the spirit after it teleports must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or be {@condition frightened} of the spirit until the end of the target's next turn.

bonus.name: Blissful Touch (Joy Only)
entries: The spirit touches one other creature within its reach. The target gains 5 temporary hit points.

reaction.name: Empathic Feedback (Joy Only)
entries: When the spirit takes damage from a creature it can see within 60 feet of it, the creature must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC, taking {@damage 2d10} psychic damage on a failed saving throw, or half as much damage on a successful one.

spell.name: Anchor Plane

m.text: a Siberys dragonshard or essence from the plane to be anchored worth at least 7,500 gp, which the spell consumes

entries: You use the material component of this spell as a planar anchor, harmonizing yourself and the area surrounding the anchor with a plane of your choice. The anchor must be placed anywhere within range, and the spell fails if you don't remain within range of the anchor during the entire casting time.

When you finish casting the spell, the anchor melds into the environment, creating a localized weakness in the planar boundary that precipitates the creation of a 5-foot-radius manifest zone tied to your chosen plane.
The fledgling zone expands in radius at the rate of 5 feet per day, to a maximum of 150 feet. Additional castings of this spell don't stack, but multiple adjacent or overlapping areas can be created.
The new manifest zone has one planar trait of your choice from the plane to which it is tied.

spell.name: Dream Link
entries: You cause yourself and all willing creatures within a 10-foot radius centered on you to enter the dreams of a target creature already dreaming within that radius. The target creature must make a Wisdom saving throw; a willing creature can voluntarily fail. On a failed saving throw, you and the other willing creatures fall asleep and telepathically enter the target's dream. This sleep counts towards any sleep necessary for completing a long rest. Affected creatures remain asleep until they finish a long rest, take any damage outside of the dream, or someone outside of the dream uses an action to shake or slap the dreaming creature awake. If the target creature wakes up, the effects of the spell immediately end, causing all creatures affected by this spell to also wake up.

While within the dream, the rules of reality and the state of the creatures affected by this spell are determined by the context of the dream itself. If the dream belongs to a player character, they can choose its contents and shape 'reality' as they wish (within the confines of what their character knows). Typically, characters dream of themselves in peak performance, with all hit points and resources as though 'waking up' from a long rest. This state does not carry over to reality if their actual long rest is interrupted. A character that "dies" in a dream immediately wakes up. A character that wakes this way takes {@damage 3d6} psychic damage, gains no benefit from their rest, and becomes unable to sleep for the next 24 hours.

If the target creature is the target of the {@spell dream} spell while it is also the target of this spell, the messenger sent by the {@spell dream} spell has less control over the shared dream entered by creatures affected by this spell than would otherwise be the case as described in the {@spell dream} spell. The messenger can interact with creatures affected by this spell as if the messenger were physically present in the dream, using its normal statistics, unless the caster of the {@spell dream} spell sent a monstrous or terrifying messenger. In that case, after the monstrous messenger delivers its short message, instead of the target of the {@spell dream} spell making a Wisdom saving throw, the DM can decide to resolve the effect of the monstrous messenger's presence with a combat encounter against the messenger inside the target's dream. The monstrous messenger can take any form the caster of the {@spell dream} spell desires, but its statistics have a challenge rating equal to no greater than twice the {@spell dream} spell's level. Defeating the messenger has the same result as succeeding on the saving throw against the {@spell dream} spell, and any creature that "dies" to the messenger in the dream suffers the effects described above.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell while already within a dream using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, all creatures affected by the original casting of the spell, including the target dreamer, are pulled into a different dream representing a deeper layer of the target dreamer's {@condition unconscious}. This can be repeated, with the required spell slot increasing by two levels for every successive layer of dream. A character who 'dies' in a deeper level of dream wakes up again in the 'shallower' level in which this spell was cast, and must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or gain a level of {@condition exhaustion}.

spell.name: Dream Lock
entries: One creature of your choice that you can see within range must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have its head wrapped in a nimbus of glowing light. Creatures who do not dream, such as kalashtar and most constructs, automatically succeed on the saving throw against this spell. The nimbus is a manifestation of the target's psychic connection to Dal Quor, the Region of Dreams, creating sensory input that overlaps the target's perceptions of reality with those of dreamspace. For the duration of the spell, the target has disadvantage on attack rolls and Wisdom checks. When the spell ends, the target's memory of the spell's duration is hazy and instinct, easily written off as a vivid daydream. slot of 2nd level or higher, you can target one additional

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell creature for each slot level above 1st. The creatures must be within 30 feet of each other when you target them.

spell.name: Dream Spirit
entries: You reach through the faded link to Dal Quor, the Region of Dreams, and for the duration of this spell you pull the spirit of a predatory, drifting dream creature through to your plane. The dream spirit remains on your current plane for the duration of this spell or until you cast this spell again and it attacks creatures you direct it to. The dream spirit does not occupy a space, and is immune to all damage.

When you cast the spell, make a melee spell attack against a creature within 5 feet of the dream spirit. On a hit, the target takes psychic damage equal to {@dice 2d6} + your spellcasting ability modifier. As a bonus action on your turn, you can move the dream spirit up to 30 feet and repeat the attack against a creature within 5 feet of it.

The dream spirit attacks by telepathically sensing a target's psychic link to Dal Quor and briefly becoming the creature's worst nightmare, but is only visible as such to the target. Otherwise, the dream spirit is visible only as a distinct haze in the air. Creatures who do not dream, such as kalashtar and most constructs, are immune to the dream spirit's attacks.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for every two slot levels above 3rd.

spell.name: Dreamwalk

duration.condition: or until the target wakes up

m.text: a translucent opal lens worth at least 500 gp

entries: You attempt to bring yourself and all willing creatures within a 10-foot radius centered on you into the dreams of a sleeping creature on the same plane as you. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC to prevent this, with the DC modified as per the table below. Additionally, on a successful saving throw, the target becomes immune to this spell for 24 hours. On a failure, you and the other willing creatures fall asleep. This sleep counts towards any sleep necessary for completing a long rest. Dreaming creatures remain asleep until they finish a long rest, take any damage outside of the dream, or someone outside of the dream uses an action to shake or slap the dreaming creature awake. If the target creature wakes up, the effects of the spell immediately end, causing all creatures affected by this spell to also wake up.
The save modifier of the target is affected by the following modifiers when the spell is cast:

rows: Secondhand
Firsthand
Familiar
Likeness or picture
Possession or garment
Body part, lock of hair, or the like

entries: While within the dream, the rules of reality and the state of the creatures affected by this spell are determined by the context of the dream itself. If the dream belongs to a player character, they can choose its contents and shape 'reality' as they wish (within the confines of what their character knows). Typically, characters dream of themselves in peak performance, with all hit points and resources as though 'waking up' from a long rest. This state does not carry over to reality if their actual long rest is interrupted. A character that 'dies' in a dream immediately wakes up. A character that wakes this way takes {@damage 3d6} psychic damage, gains no benefit from their rest, and becomes unable to sleep for the next 24 hours.

If the target creature is the target of the {@spell dream} spell while it is also the target of this spell, this spell bars the messenger's entry into the target's dream, causing the {@spell dream} spell to fail.

entries.name: Entering Dreams and Non-Dreamers
entries: The spells {@spell dream link|TalvakrisGuidetoAdar} and {@spell dreamwalk|TalvakrisGuidetoAdar} allow characters to enter the dreams of others. Characters who don't dream, such as kalashtar, cannot be the target of these spells, but they can cast them or be among the willing creatures that enter the dream of the spell's target. This is because the caster and affected creatures are not dreaming, they are only magically linked telepathically to the target; it is only the target that truly dreams for the duration of the spell's effects.

spell.name: Dimensional Anchor
entries: You fire a green ray from your outstretched hand toward one creature or object that you can see within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target is covered with a shimmering emerald field that completely blocks extradimensional travel. Forms of travel barred by this spell include all teleportation effects and spells and abilities that allow the target to transport to a different plane, demiplane, or extradimensional space, such as {@spell astral projection}, {@spell banishment}, {@spell demiplane}, {@spell etherealness}, {@spell gate}, {@spell maze}, {@spell Mordenkainen's magnificent mansion}, and {@spell plane shift}. For the duration of the spell, an affected target can make a Charisma saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the spell on a success.

spell.name: Phantasmal Horror
entries: You project a primal existential terror from the primordial recesses of sentience itself. Each creature in a 90-foot cone perceives this ineffable visage of annihilating horror and must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, a target takes {@damage 10d10} psychic damage and becomes {@condition frightened} until the end of its next turn, or until you stop {@status concentration||concentrating}. On a success, a target takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition frightened}.

At the end of its next turn, a creature that is still {@condition frightened} by this spell must repeat the saving throw. On a failure, the creature dies if it has less than half its hit points, or its hit points drop to 0 if it had half or more of its hit points. On a success, the creature is {@condition frightened} for the duration of this spell.

spell.name: Phantasmal Nightmare
entries: You tap into the nightmares of a creature you can see within range and create an illusory manifestation of its deepest fears, visible only to that creature. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the target takes {@damage 4d10} psychic damage and becomes {@condition frightened} until the end of its next turn, or until you stop {@status concentration||concentrating}. On a success, the target takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition frightened}.

At the end of its next turn, a creature that is still {@condition frightened} by this spell must repeat the saving throw. On a failure, the creature's hit points drop to 0. On a success, the creature is {@condition frightened} for the duration of this spell.

spell.name: Hostbind

components.m: a Khyber shard worth a number of gp equal to the possessing creature's challenge multiplied by 100

entries: When you cast this spell, you target a possessed creature within range and make a melee spell attack against it. On a hit, you touch this spell's material component to the target, which magically embeds itself in the target's body for the duration of the spell. While the Khyber shard is embedded in the target, the possessing creature becomes unable to leave the target's body, and the life force of the target and the creature possessing it become inexorably linked. While so linked, if the target dies, the Khyber shard embedded in the target and the possessing creature are both instantly destroyed. At the end of each of the target's turns, the possessing creature may make a Charisma save, ending the spell's effect on the target on a success. If the spell ends without the destruction of the possessing creature, the Khyber shard is ejected from the target's body, intact.

Many possessing entities from other planes of existence are tied to the energy of their plane, and so are never permanently destroyed. For example, this spell could destroy the personality and memories of a particular quori possessing an Inspired, but that quori's energy returns to Dal Quor to eventually form a new quori of the same kind. However, some powerful entities may retain some or all of their memories upon reforming. Even if this is the case, a destroyed entity typically takes {@dice 2d10} weeks to reform.

spell.name: Psychic Reformation
entries: You guide a target creature within range through meditation to psychically reorganize its memories and past experiences, following different paths through its life that it may have taken. You must remain in contact with the target and the target must concentrate (as if {@status concentration||concentrating} on a spell) for the entire casting time, or the spell fails.
When you finish casting this spell, the target creature can choose to do any of the following:

items: Lose proficiency in one skill and gain proficiency in another.
Lose proficiency in one tool it is proficient with and gain proficiency in another.
Forget one language it knows and learn another.
Change any decisions it made for any Ability Score Improvement class feature it previously gained, including the decision to take a feat it meets the prerequisites for, if applicable.
Forget a spell known and choose a different one of the same level.

entries: The target can choose to do any of the above effects any number of times, however each time an effect is chosen, the maximum hit point total of both you and the target are reduced by 2. This reduction lasts for a year or until the affected creature gains a level, whichever comes first, for each creature respectively. It cannot be restored with greater restoration or similar magic.

spell.name: Microcosm
entries: Each creature with 100 hit points or fewer of your choice within range must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be affected by this spell. If only one valid target is within range, the target makes this save with disadvantage.

An affected target's mind is cut off from all of its senses. Instead of the real world, affected targets perceive a hallucinatory environment that appears and feels real to them. If multiple targets are affected, they telepathically share the same hallucinatory environment and can perceive, feel, communicate, and interact with each other as if they were actually present in this imaginary landscape. Affected targets believe this hallucinatory environment, and other creatures they meet in it, are absolutely real.

However, in reality affected targets immediately fall {@condition prone}, and are {@condition incapacitated} and {@condition unconscious}. Such creatures will eventually die of thirst and starvation without external care.

This spell's effects can only be removed by casting microcosm on affected targets a second time, or other powerful effects such as divine intervention, {@spell wish}, or a {@spell greater restoration} spell cast at 9th level.

entries.name: Double
entries: A double weapon has two striking ends on either side of an elongated grip. It is too unwieldy for Small creatures to handle properly; they have disadvantage on attack rolls with double-ended weapons. When you use your {@action Attack} action to make an attack with a double-ended weapon, you can also use a bonus action to make an additional attack with it. You don't add your ability modifier to the damage of the bonus action attack, unless that modifier is negative.
If you have the {@optfeature Two-Weapon Fighting} fighting style or {@feat Dual Wielder} feat, you gain all of their benefits while wielding a double weapon, as if it were two different weapons.

entries.name: Psiactive
entries: A psiactive ranged weapon draws its power from your innate psionic potential, whether or not you're trained to use it. When attacking with a psiactive weapon, you use your choice of your Intelligence or Charisma modifier for the attack and damage rolls.

You can fire psiactive weapons a number of times equal to twice your proficiency bonus. After that, your psionic potential is temporarily expended and you can't fire another psiactive weapon until you regain your psionic focus by using an action or a bonus action (your choice). Your psionic focus is shared between all psiactive weapons; whenever you make an attack with any psiactive weapon, you expend one of your uses.

As psiactive weapons channel your own latent energy, they aren't considered magic weapons and they can't be sensed with the {@spell detect magic} spell or similar magic. However, they don't function in an {@spell antimagic field}.

entries.name: Special
entries: When you wield a katar, it fits around your hand, making it hard to disarm you of it. You have advantage on any ability check or saving throw to resist being disarmed of or dropping a katar you wield. However, it requires a bonus action to draw or stow a katar. While wielding a katar in a hand, you can't use that hand for anything else.

entries.name: Special
entries: When you make a ranged attack with a nonmagical cutting wheel and miss, it returns to you at the end of your turn. A nonmagical cutting wheel can be caught with a DC 10 Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check. A magical cutting wheel you've made a ranged attack with returns to you at the end of your turn, regardless of whether you hit or miss, and it can be caught automatically.
If you fail the {@skill Acrobatics} check or you do not have a hand free to catch the returning cutting wheel, it falls to the ground 10 feet away from you in a random direction.

entries.name: Special
entries: When wielding a zulaat, at the start of your turn, you must choose for the weapon to have either the {@itemProperty R||Reach} or the {@itemProperty TGA:DB|TalvakrisGuidetoAdar|Double} weapon property until the start of your next turn. While a zulaat has the {@itemProperty R||Reach} property, it deals {@damage 2d4} slashing damage on a hit, and while it has the {@itemProperty TGA:DB|TalvakrisGuidetoAdar|Double} property, it deals {@damage 1d6} slashing damage on a hit.

item.name: Dorje of Blasting
entries: This crystalline wand is 14 inches long and made from a single crystal. It has 7 charges. While at least one charge remains, it glows faintly with purple energy, shedding {@quickref Vision and Light||2||dim light} in a 5-foot radius. While holding the wand, you can use an action and expend 1 or more of the wand's charges to target one creature you can see within 60 feet of you. The wand and the target's eyes flash the same color of light, and the target takes {@damage 2d6} psychic damage for every charge you expended.
The wand regains {@dice 1d6 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the wand's last charge, roll a {@dice d20}. On a 1, the wand shatters, crumbling into crystalline dust and is destroyed.

item.name: Dorje of Broadcasting
entries: This crystalline wand is 9 inches long and made from a single crystal. It has 7 charges. While at least one charge remains, it glows faintly with blue energy, shedding {@quickref Vision and Light||2||dim light} in a 5-foot radius. While holding the wand, you can use an action to expend 1 or more of its charges to send a short telepathic message of up to 25 words to every creature within 500 feet of you. Creatures you can see, as well as creatures that you know or know of in the area, can be included or excluded from this telepathic communication. The communication is one-way only, instantaneous, and recipients know it came from you. If a recipient of your telepathic message does not share a language with you, it perceives only garbled mental gibberish.
The wand regains {@dice 1d6 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the wand's last charge, roll a {@dice d20}. On a 1, the wand shatters, crumbling into crystalline dust and is destroyed.

item.name: Dream Silk Robe
entries: This beautiful multicolored silk robe is made of crystalline threads that have been treated and woven into a special form of sentira known as dream silk. Like all forms of sentira, it is both lightweight and surprisingly strong, even in fabric form. If you aren't wearing armor, your base Armor Class is 11 + your Dexterity modifier while wearing this robe.

item.name: Emotion Vault
entries: This sentira amulet contains {@dice 1d4} small gemstones. While attuned to the amulet, when you experience a bout of strong emotion, you can choose to store the feeling in one of the amulet's gems. If all gems are full when you go to store an emotion, the oldest stored emotion is replaced. Touching a gemstone reveals the emotion and approximate situation experienced by the person who stored this feeling, such a 'joy from a festival', but specific details are not given.
If you are shaping sentira while attuned to the emotion vault, you can use a stored emotion to give the sentira color. Doing this releases the stored emotion, emptying the gem.

item.name: Mind's Eye
entries: This small gem has one large, flat facet. When you attune to a mind's eye and press the large flat facet against the middle of your forehead, it affixes itself there and cannot be removed by anyone other than you while you are conscious.

While a mind's eye is affixed to your forehead, it grants you mystic vision, allowing you to see all creatures within 30 feet of you, including hidden and {@condition invisible} ones, regardless of lighting conditions. You may take an action to expand the mystic vision of your mind's eye, granting you the ability to see through objects that are up to 1 foot thick within 30 feet of you for up to 1 minute. Once you expand the mystic vision of the mind's eye in this way, it cannot be expanded again until the next dawn.

item.name: Speaker's Dais
entries: The Speaker's Dais is a stone platform placed in the center of the Council Hall amphitheater in Kashta Keep. Unlike most magic items, up to a dozen qualified creatures can attune to the Speaker's Dais at the same time. A collective of nine attuned individuals can perform a special ritual that lasts 1 hour to break the attunement of any other individual attuned to the Speaker's Dais. (This is an extremely rare punishment reserved only for those who have abused their positions of leadership within Kashta Keep, last used to break Raadu Xeel's attunement after his involvement with the Endseekers was discovered and before he was sentenced to execution.)
The dais has 50 charges for the following properties. It regains {@dice 4d6 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Spell Negation
entries: While you are within 90 feet of the dais, you have advantage on saving throws against spells. In addition, when another creature casts a spell within 90 feet of the dais, you can expend a number of charges equal to the spell's level and use your reaction. If you do, the dais nullifies the magic of the spell, canceling its effect.

entries.name: Spells
entries: While standing upon the dais, you can use an action to expend 1 or more of its charges to cast one of the following spells from it, using your spell save DC: {@spell calm emotions} (2 charges), {@spell command} (1 charge), dimensional anchor (4 charges; new spell, see Chapter 12), {@spell hold monster} (5 charges), {@spell sleep} (3rd-level version, 3 charges), {@spell tongues} (3 charges), or {@spell zone of truth} (2 charges).

You can also use an action to cast one of the following spells from the dais while standing on it, without using any charges: {@spell detect magic}, {@spell light}, {@spell mage hand}, or {@spell sending}.

item.name: Sentira Potion Bottle
entries: This yellow-tinted bottle is made from Irian quartz and sentira infused with vigor and warm thoughts. The sentira imbues its magic into any kind of {@item potion of healing} that is poured into it. If the potion remains sealed in the bottle for 1 week before it is imbibed, the drinker of the potion regains the maximum amount of hit points that kind of {@item potion of healing} can restore.

item.name: Sentira Shard (Arrogance)
entries: For this shard's properties to work, it must be attached to a {@filter sentira lens weapon|items|source=|type=!treasure (gemstone);!treasure (art object);!renaissance;ranged weapon;!treasure (coinage);!modern;!futuristic;!trade bar|miscellaneous=|search=sentira lens} (see Weapons). You can attach it or remove it from a sentira lens with 10 minutes of work. A sentira lens can only have one sentira shard attached at a time.

You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this shard's sentira lens. Additionally, this shard has 3 charges, and it regains all expended charges daily at dawn. When you hit a creature with an attack using this shard's sentira lens, you can expend 1 charge to cloud the target's judgment with a false sense of pride and invincibility. The target must succeed on a DC 17 Wisdom saving throw or become immune to being {@condition frightened} for 1 minute. For the duration, the target has disadvantage on saving throws against being {@condition charmed} and is vulnerable to psychic damage, but doesn't know when it takes psychic damage.

item.name: Sentira Shard (Vim)
entries: For this shard's properties to work, it must be attached to a {@filter sentira lens weapon|items|source=|type=!treasure (gemstone);!treasure (art object);!renaissance;ranged weapon;!treasure (coinage);!modern;!futuristic;!trade bar|miscellaneous=|search=sentira lens} (see Weapons). You can attach it or remove it from a sentira lens with 10 minutes of work. A sentira lens can only have one sentira shard attached at a time.

You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this shard's sentira lens. Additionally, this shard has 3 charges, and it regains all expended charges daily at dawn. When you hit a creature with an attack using this shard's sentira lens, you can expend 1 charge to deal no damage to the target and instead fill the target with vigor and enthusiasm. The target gains an amount of temporary hit points equal to what your damage with the attack would have been. These temporary hit points last until the target finishes a short or long rest.

item.name: Sentira Shard (Wrath)
entries: For this shard's properties to work, it must be attached to a {@filter sentira lens weapon|items|source=|type=!treasure (gemstone);!treasure (art object);!renaissance;ranged weapon;!treasure (coinage);!modern;!futuristic;!trade bar|miscellaneous=|search=sentira lens} (see Weapons). You can attach it or remove it from a sentira lens with 10 minutes of work. A sentira lens can only have one sentira shard attached at a time.

You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this shard's sentira lens. Additionally, this shard has 3 charges, and it regains all expended charges daily at dawn. When you hit a creature with an attack using this shard's sentira lens, you can expend 1 or more charges to ignite the target's anger and cause them to spontaneously combust. The target takes {@damage 2d6} fire damage for every charge you expended.

item.name: Sentira Staff
entries: This {@item quarterstaff|PHB} is made from sentira. Elite soldiers of the Harmonious Shield—the Unity of Riedra's army—are often outfitted with these weapons.

While holding the weapon, you may use a bonus action to cause blades of psychic energy to simultaneously extend or retract from both ends of it. While these blades are extended, the weapon becomes a zulaat, and it deals psychic damage instead of slashing damage in this form.
If you are proficient with quarterstaves, you are proficient with a sentira {@item staff|PHB} in zulaat form.
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, no matter what form it is in.

item.name: Skill Shards
entries: These tiny slivers of psycho-conductive crystal are suffused with a splinter of knowledge or aptitude. Each skill shard is synced to a skill. You may crush a shard as a bonus action to activate it, destroying the shard. Once activated, you may apply twice your proficiency bonus on the next ability check you make using the shard's synced skill instead of any proficiency bonus you might normally apply, provided you make the ability check within the next minute.

item.name: Torc of Free Will
entries: This crysteel band inlaid with Siberys dragonshard gems can be worn around your neck or upper arm. While attuned to and wearing it, you are immune to any effect that, on a failed Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma saving throw, randomly determines or dictates your actions or allows another creature to command you; such as the gaze of an umber hulk or the {@spell command}, {@spell confusion}, {@spell dominate}, or {@spell suggestion} spells.

item.name: Sentira Figurine of Wondrous Power, Dread Panther
entries: A sentira figurine of wondrous power is a statuette of a beast small enough to fit in a pocket, made out of sentira saturated with a particular emotion. If you use an action to focus on the emotion keyed to the figurine and throw it to a point on the ground within 60 feet of you, the figurine becomes a living creature. If the space where the creature would appear is occupied by other creatures or objects, or if there isn't enough space for the creature, the figurine doesn't become a creature.

The creature is friendly to you and your companions. It understands your languages and obeys your mental or spoken commands. If you issue no commands, the creature defends itself but takes no other actions.

The creature exists for a duration specific to each figurine. At the end of the duration, the creature reverts to its figurine form. It reverts to a figurine early if it drops to 0 hit points or if you use an action to mentally command it to revert while touching it. When the creature becomes a figurine again, its property can't be used again until a certain amount of time has passed, as specified in the figurine's description.

entries.name: Dread Panther (Rare)
entries: This matte-black sentira statuette is sculpted in the likeness of a stalking panther. It can become a {@creature tiger} (see the Monster Manual) that is jetblack in coloration for up to 1 hour. Once the figurine has been used, it can't be used again until 7 days have passed. The tiger has {@sense darkvision} out to a radius of 60 feet and advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide in {@quickref Vision and Light||2||dim light} or darkness.

item.name: Sentira Figurine of Wondrous Power, Fury Badger
entries: A sentira figurine of wondrous power is a statuette of a beast small enough to fit in a pocket, made out of sentira saturated with a particular emotion. If you use an action to focus on the emotion keyed to the figurine and throw it to a point on the ground within 60 feet of you, the figurine becomes a living creature. The creature exists for a duration specific to each figurine. At the end of the duration, the creature reverts to its figurine form. It reverts to a figurine early if it drops to 0 hit points or if you use an action to mentally command it to revert while touching it. When the creature becomes a figurine again, its property can't be used again until a certain amount of time has passed, as specified in the figurine's description.

entries.name: Fury Badger (Rare)
entries: This vivid crimson sentira statuette is sculpted in the likeness of a ferocious-looking badger with its hackles raised. It can become a Medium badger for up to 12 hours. Once it has been used, it can't be used again until 5 days have passed. The badger has the same statistics as a {@creature hell hound} (see the Monster Manual), except that it has a burrowing speed of 30 feet.
If you command the figurine to stop fighting or nonlethally defeat an enemy, it has a {@chance 25} chance to ignore that order and all other orders until it reverts to figurine form.

item.name: Sentira Figurine of Wondrous Power, Gloom Vulture
entries: A sentira figurine of wondrous power is a statuette of a beast small enough to fit in a pocket, made out of sentira saturated with a particular emotion. If you use an action to focus on the emotion keyed to the figurine and throw it to a point on the ground within 60 feet of you, the figurine becomes a living creature. If the space where the creature would appear is occupied by other creatures or objects, or if there isn't enough space for the creature, the figurine doesn't become a creature.

The creature exists for a duration specific to each figurine. At the end of the duration, the creature reverts to its figurine form. It reverts to a figurine early if it drops to 0 hit points or if you use an action to mentally command it to revert while touching it. When the creature becomes a figurine again, its property can't be used again until a certain amount of time has passed, as specified in the figurine's description.

entries.name: Gloom Vulture (Very Rare)
entries: This dull gray sentira statuette is sculpted in the likeness of a disheveled vulture. It can become a {@creature giant vulture} (see the Monster Manual) for up to 3 hours. Once it is used, it can't be used again until 13 days have passed. In addition, the vulture radiates a 30-foot-radius aura of despair while it flies. Any creature hostile to you that starts its turn beneath the vulture and in the aura must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition incapacitated} and have its speed halved for 1 minute, or until the vulture reverts to figurine form. The affected creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Once it successfully saves against the effect, a creature is immune to the vulture's aura for the next 24 hours.

item.name: Sentira Figurine of Wondrous Power, Joy Rabbit
entries: A sentira figurine of wondrous power is a statuette of a beast small enough to fit in a pocket, made out of sentira saturated with a particular emotion. If you use an action to focus on the emotion keyed to the figurine and throw it to a point on the ground within 60 feet of you, the figurine becomes a living creature. If the space where the creature would appear is occupied by other creatures or objects, or if there isn't enough space for the creature, the figurine doesn't become a creature.

The creature is friendly to you and your companions. It understands your languages and obeys your mental or spoken commands. If you issue no commands, the creature defends itself but takes no other actions.

The creature exists for a duration specific to each figurine. At the end of the duration, the creature reverts to its figurine form. It reverts to a figurine early if it drops to 0 hit points or if you use an action to mentally command it to revert while touching it. When the creature becomes a figurine again, its property can't be used again until a certain amount of time has passed, as specified in the figurine's description.

entries.name: Joy Rabbit (Uncommon)
entries: This bright yellow sentira statuette is sculpted in the likeness of a rotund, fluffy bunny. It can become a {@creature giant rabbit} (see Chapter 13) for up to 12 hours. Once it has been used, it can't be used again until 2 days have passed. While in giant rabbit form, the figurine can use the Dash or Disengage actions as a bonus action on each of its turns, and it can cast {@spell goodberry} once, using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability.

item.name: Sentira Figurine of Wondrous Power, Pride Monkey
entries: A sentira figurine of wondrous power is a statuette of a beast small enough to fit in a pocket, made out of sentira saturated with a particular emotion. If you use an action to focus on the emotion keyed to the figurine and throw it to a point on the ground within 60 feet of you, the figurine becomes a living creature. If the space where the creature would appear is occupied by other creatures or objects, or if there isn't enough space for the creature, the figurine doesn't become a creature.

The creature is friendly to you and your companions. It understands your languages and obeys your mental or spoken commands. If you issue no commands, the creature defends itself but takes no other actions.

The creature exists for a duration specific to each figurine. At the end of the duration, the creature reverts to its figurine form. It reverts to a figurine early if it drops to 0 hit points or if you use an action to mentally command it to revert while touching it. When the creature becomes a figurine again, its property can't be used again until a certain amount of time has passed, as specified in the figurine's description.

entries.name: Pride Monkey (Legendary)
entries: This deep purple sentira statuette is sculpted in the likeness of a monkey posed in a martial artist's stance. It can become a Medium anthropomorphic monkey for up to 24 hours. Once the figurine has been used, it can't be used again until 12 days have passed. The monkey has the same statistics as a {@creature martial arts master|TalvakrisGuidetoAdar} (see Chapter 13), except that it is a beast instead of a humanoid.

The figurine has a 60 percent chance each time you use it to ignore your commands, including a command to revert to figurine form. Even if it ignores your commands, the figurine remains friendly to you and your companions.

item.name: Sentira Figurine of Wondrous Power, Serenity Horses
entries: A sentira figurine of wondrous power is a statuette of a beast small enough to fit in a pocket, made out of sentira saturated with a particular emotion. If you use an action to focus on the emotion keyed to the figurine and throw it to a point on the ground within 60 feet of you, the figurine becomes a living creature. If the space where the creature would appear is occupied by other creatures or objects, or if there isn't enough space for the creature, the figurine doesn't become a creature.

The creature is friendly to you and your companions. It understands your languages and obeys your mental or spoken commands. If you issue no commands, the creature defends itself but takes no other actions.

The creature exists for a duration specific to each figurine. At the end of the duration, the creature reverts to its figurine form. It reverts to a figurine early if it drops to 0 hit points or if you use an action to mentally command it to revert while touching it. When the creature becomes a figurine again, its property can't be used again until a certain amount of time has passed, as specified in the figurine's description.

entries.name: Serenity Horses (Rare)
entries: These aquamarine sentira statuettes are sculpted in the likenesses of two horses mid-stride, and are always created in pairs. Each horse in the pair looks unique. You can use one figurine or both simultaneously. One figurine can become an {@creature asperi|TalvakrisGuidetoAdar}, and the other can become a {@creature rendesa|TalvakrisGuidetoAdar} (see Chapter 13 for the statistics of both creatures). Each figurine's transformation lasts 6 hours. Once a horse figurine has been used, it can't be used again until 7 days have passed.

item.name: Thoughtweaver's Tools
entries: This toolkit is a kind of {@item artisan's tools|PHB}, costs 50 gp, and weighs 5 pounds. Sentira workers use thoughtweaver's tools to pull and shape sentira into everything from works of art to building materials.

entries.name: Components
entries: The tools include a small circlet to be worn on the head, a thin blowpipe, and tweezers. You need powdered crystal to shape sentira as per Shaping Sentira

entries.name: Arcana, History
entries: You are well attuned to the processes of making sentira. You can identify the emotion or emotions used to make a sentira object, and you are able to identify its culture of origin.

entries.name: Sense Memory
entries: By touching a sentira object for one minute, you can pick up details of the memory used to make a sentira object. For instance, you may learn that a shield was made with the memory of a particularly difficult battle.

itemGroup.name: Sentira Figurine of Wondrous Power
items: Sentira Figurine of Wondrous Power, Dread Panther|TalvakrisGuidetoAdar
Sentira Figurine of Wondrous Power, Fury Badger|TalvakrisGuidetoAdar
Sentira Figurine of Wondrous Power, Gloom Vulture|TalvakrisGuidetoAdar
Sentira Figurine of Wondrous Power, Joy Rabbit|TalvakrisGuidetoAdar
Sentira Figurine of Wondrous Power, Pride Monkey|TalvakrisGuidetoAdar
Sentira Figurine of Wondrous Power, Serenity Horses|TalvakrisGuidetoAdar

entries: A rare crystalline mineral mined in the northern reaches of Riedra and the far-off continent of Xen'drik can be alloyed with steel, resulting in this glittering, vaguely chromatic metal.

A weapon made of crysteel is not inherently magical, but if the wielder possesses enough supernatural energy, the weapon can focus that power into telekinetic force. If you can cast spells and have at least one 3rd-level or higher spell still available to cast, a crysteel weapon deals force damage instead of its normal damage type when you use it to make a weapon attack.
Alternatively, if you have the Ki-Empowered Strikes feature and you wield a monk weapon made of crysteel, it always deals force damage when you use it to make a weapon attack.
The crysteel version of a melee weapon costs 1,000 gp more than the normal version.

Sentira is an opalescent substance with an organic appearance similar to horn or shell. It is not psionically potent on its own, but it responds to psionically potent thoughts and emotions. The Unity of Riedra produces the vast majority of sentira equipment, structures, and monuments, though Adarans create art, arms, and armor from it at an artisanal scale.

Weapons with the {@itemProperty T||thrown} property and {@itemProperty A||ammunition} have their normal and long ranges doubled when made from sentira. Melee weapons that do not normally have the {@itemProperty H||heavy} property gain the {@itemProperty F||finesse} property when made from sentira. Melee weapons that do normally have the {@itemProperty H||heavy} property do not have it when made from sentira.
The sentira version of a melee weapon or ten pieces of sentira ammunition costs 400 gp more than the normal versions.

entries.name: Producing and Shaping Sentira
entries: Sentira is a manufactured material, one that anyone with psionic training can produce with enough time. To produce and shape sentira, you must be proficient in {@item thoughtweaver's tools|TalvakrisGuidetoAdar} (see Tools) or know the cantrip {@spell mage hand}, and you must expend one of the following: a spell slot of 1st level or higher, 1 ki point, or 1 hit die. Over the course of 1 hour, you can concentrate on this ritual (as though {@status concentration||concentrating} on a spell) to turn 1 pound of powdered crystal into 1 pound of sentira. You can extend this ritual for up to 8 hours, producing an additional pound of sentira for each hour of work.

While shaping sentira, you can choose to infuse the sentira with a strong emotion either from yourself or from an emotion stored in an item such as an emotion vault. This colors the otherwise clear or pearly white sentira. You can shape the sentira into any mundane object that does not have complex mechanisms, such a spyglass or crossbow.
