To produce and shape sentira, you must be proficient in {@item thoughtweaver's tools|TalvakrisGuidetoAdar} (see Tools) or know the cantrip {@spell mage hand}, and you must expend one of the following: a spell slot of 1st level or higher, 1 ki point, or 1 hit die. Over the course of 1 hour, you can concentrate on this ritual (as though {@status concentration||concentrating} on a spell) to turn 1 pound of powdered crystal into 1 pound of sentira. You can extend this ritual for up to 8 hours, producing an additional pound of sentira for each hour of work.

While shaping sentira, you can choose to infuse the sentira with a strong emotion either from yourself or from an emotion stored in an item such as an emotion vault. This colors the otherwise clear or pearly white sentira. You can shape the sentira into any mundane object that does not have complex mechanisms, such a spyglass or crossbow.

Sentira that is shaped into the form of armor is exceptionally lightweight and sturdy. A sentira breastplate or half-plate can be worn under normal clothes. Sentira half-plate and plate don't have disadvantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks, and sentira plate armor does not have a Strength requirement.

Sentira that is shaped into the form of armor is exceptionally lightweight and sturdy. A sentira breastplate or half-plate can be worn under normal clothes. Sentira half-plate and plate don't have disadvantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks, and sentira plate armor does not have a Strength requirement.

entries.name: Calm
entries: This sentira armor is a deep, oceanic blue in color.

While attuned to and wearing this armor, you have a {=bonusAc} bonus to Armor Class, are immune to being {@condition frightened}, and you have advantage on all Constitution saving throws to maintain {@status concentration}.

Sentira that is shaped into the form of armor is exceptionally lightweight and sturdy. A sentira breastplate or half-plate can be worn under normal clothes. Sentira half-plate and plate don't have disadvantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks, and sentira plate armor does not have a Strength requirement.

entries.name: Dread
entries: This sentira armor is matte black, lacking the usual opalescent luster typical of shaped sentira. While attuned to and wearing this armor, you have a +1 bonus to Armor Class and whenever a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with a melee attack and deals damage, you can use your reaction to force the creature to roll a {@dice d6} and subtract the result from its damage roll (dealing a minimum of 1 damage). Creatures immune to being {@condition frightened} are unaffected by this property.

Sentira that is shaped into the form of armor is exceptionally lightweight and sturdy. A sentira breastplate or half-plate can be worn under normal clothes. Sentira half-plate and plate don't have disadvantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks, and sentira plate armor does not have a Strength requirement.

entries.name: Wrath
entries: This sentira armor is the exact crimson shade of freshly spilled blood, and always appears wet (though it is not). While attuned to and wearing this armor, you have a +1 bonus to Armor Class and when you take damage from a creature, your next successful melee attack against that creature before the end of your next turn deals an extra {@damage 1d8} psychic damage.

When you hit with an attack using this magic weapon, the target takes an extra {@damage 1d6} lightning damage and {@damage 1d6} thunder damage. If the target is outdoors in stormy conditions, it takes an additional {@damage 1d6} lightning damage and {@damage 1d6} thunder damage.

Endseekers will sometimes make sentira armor made from the boiling rage of the Unmaker. This armor can only be reliably made near Korrandar, and it is vanishingly rare. The armor is made of deep red sentira and is hot to the touch. If the armor normally imposes disadvantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks or has a Strength requirement, this version does not. An Endseeker breastplate or Endseeker half-plate can be worn under normal clothes.

Endseeker armor has 10 charges. While attuned to the armor, you can use an action to expend 1 or more of its charges to cast one of the following spells from it: {@spell inflict wounds} (1 charge per spell level, up to 4th), {@spell disintegrate} (6 charges). The armor regains {@dice 1d4} charges every dawn.
When you cast spells from this armor, you have {=bonusWeaponAttack} to hit with spell attacks and a spell save DC of 16.

entries.name: Curse
entries: Endseeker armor is cursed. Attuning to it curses you until you are targeted by the {@spell greater restoration} spell or similar magic. After attuning to Endseeker armor, you cannot remove or unattune from it. When you are reduced to 0 hit points or die, or if you expend the armor's last charge, you immediately cast {@spell disintegrate} on yourself and every creature within 5 feet of you, requiring no action.
You automatically fail the saving throw. The armor then crumbles away into ash.

Sets of linked armor are created together, a sliver from a single shattered psychoactive crystal incorporated into each suit of armor in the set. Up to six suits of armor may be created in a set of linked armors. Individual suits of armor in the same set do not need to be the same kind of armor.

While attuned to and wearing a suit of linked armor, you may communicate telepathically with any or all other creatures wearing other suits of armor in the same set, and you know the direction and distance between you and each other suit of linked armor in the set. The link between sets of linked armor functions across any physical distance as long as a suit is on the same plane of existence.
When attuned to and wearing a suit of phasing armor, you can activate it as a bonus action to turn yourself and your equipment incorporeal until the end of your current turn.

While incorporeal, you may move through other creatures and objects as if they were {@quickref difficult terrain||3}. If you end your turn or run out of incorporeal movement inside an object, you are shunted aside into the nearest unoccupied space, taking {@damage 1d10} force damage for every 5 feet you are moved.

You may move a total of 120 feet while incorporeal using this armor. Once you have done so, you automatically become corporeal. Every morning at dawn, the total distance the armor lets you move incorporeally resets.

feat.name: Crescent Moons Style

entries.name: Repeatable:
entries: No
You wield cutting wheels with the grace and inevitability of the moons. You gain the following benefits:

items: When you use a cutting wheel to make a melee weapon attack, its damage die changes from a {@dice d6} to a {@dice d8}.
You may draw a cutting wheel as part of making a ranged weapon attack.
When you use a cutting wheel to make a ranged weapon attack, its short range increases to 60 feet, and its long range increases to 120 feet.

Any cutting wheel you've made a ranged attack with, whether it is magical or not, returns to you at the end of your turn, regardless of whether you hit or miss, and you can catch it automatically (provided you have a hand free).

feat.name: Dragon Guillotine Dervish

entries.name: Repeatable:
entries: No
You have mastered the longtou zha, wielding it with a fluidity that belies its weight and power. You gain the following benefits:

items: You may use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier for attack and damage rolls you make with a longtou zha.
If you have the Martial Arts feature, a longtou zha is a monk weapon for you.
You may ignore the two-handed property when wielding a longtou zha.
When you use a longtou zha, its damage die changes from a d10 to a d6 when wielded with only one hand, and from a d10 to a d12 when wielded with two hands.
When you use the Attack action to make at least one attack with a longtou zha using two hands, you can take a bonus action to make another attack with the longtou zha using one hand.

feat.name: Harmonious Balance Sentinel

entries.name: Repeatable:
entries: No
You know the secret arts of wielding the zulaat. You gain the following benefits:

items: You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls you make with a zulaat.
When you wield a zulaat, at the start of your turn, you can choose for it to have both the Reach and Double properties, instead of only one or the other.
When you use a zulaat, its damage die is always 2d4, no matter what properties it currently has.
You cannot be surprised as long as you are conscious.
You may use your Wisdom modifier instead of your Dexterity modifier when rolling for Initiative.

feat.name: Mercy of a Thousand Cuts

entries.name: Repeatable:
entries: No
You are deadlier with a pair of katars than most are with a greataxe. You gain the following benefits:

items: Drawing or stowing a katar requires no more effort for you than drawing or stowing a dagger.
You can draw or stow two katars when you would normally be able to draw or stow only one weapon.
When you use a katar, its damage die changes from a d4 to a d6.

When you hit the same creature with at least two attacks made with two different katars on the same turn, you can use your reaction to force the target to make a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength or Dexterity modifier). On a failure, it loses 1d6 hit points at the start of each of its turns until it succeeds on a Wisdom (Medicine) check against the same DC, regains all hit points, or drops to 0. Creatures are immune for 24 hours after being affected.

feat.name: Sky-Splitting Comet

entries.name: Repeatable:
entries: No
The deceptive deadliness of the meteor hammer reaches its full potential in your hands. You gain the following benefits:

items: You may use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier for attack and damage rolls with a meteor hammer.
If you have the Martial Arts feature, a meteor hammer is a monk weapon for you.
When you use a meteor hammer, its damage die changes from a d8 to a d12.
When you make a melee attack with a meteor hammer on your turn, you can use your reaction to increase your reach by 20 feet (to 30 feet total) until the start of your next turn.

feat.name: Wild Talent

entries.name: Repeatable:
entries: No
Your natural psionic talent awakens:

items: You learn the {@spell mage hand} and {@spell message} cantrips and the {@spell dissonant whispers} spell.
These spells require no spell components when cast via this feat.
Your mage hand is invisible.
Message is telepathic and requires no pointing.
You can cast dissonant whispers once per long rest without a slot, or with spell slots.

_.special: snipping of a plant that never withers

items.name: Skill Proficiencies:
items.entry: {@skill Animal Handling}, {@skill Nature}

items.name: Tool Proficiencies:
items.entry: {@item Cartographer's tools|PHB}

items.name: Languages:
items.entry: {@language Primordial}

items.name: Suggested 1st level bonus feat:
items.entry: {@feat Tough}

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: A {@item quarterstaff|PHB}, a snipping of a plant that never withers, a set of {@item traveler's clothes|PHB}, and a belt {@item pouch|PHB} containing 10 {@item gold|PHB}.

entries.name: All Walks of Life
entries: Ahydratmin is a truly ancient being, one that has imparted knowledge of the ages to humanoids, dragons, and immortals alike. There is no rhyme nor reason as to who is given an audience with them. Who were you before? Are you still that person now? Have you abandoned your life for a new purpose, or do you try and balance that with the duties of your patron? You can select a Previous Occupation below or roll randomly, then add details to determine just the type of person Ahdryatmin chose to bless.

entries.name: Feature: Lamannia's Touch
entries: You have an affinity with the natural world and the creatures within, including elementals. You can roughly understand what beasts are trying to convey, and animals are overall less hostile towards you unless provoked. Elementals are more inclined to listen to you, consider you as an ally, and come to your aid.

entries.caption: Previous Occupations
rows: Summit Road guide
Monastic scholar
Village artisan
Lightbringer
Riedran refugee
A wandering criminal

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Those chosen by the Mountainsoul are eclectic at best. Are you excited by the prospect of a grand adventure? Do you dread the road ahead? Would you rather go back to the life you once had, or are you happy to start anew?

entries.caption: Personality Traits
rows: I feel more at home with plants and animals than people; they're much better communicators too.
Life on the road scares me, even after all I've seen.
I seem more confident to friends and loved ones.
I search for hidden clues of the world everywhere I go.
I'm obsessed with uncovering the truth, no matter the cost.
I have experienced enlightenment and want to share my findings with others.

entries.caption: Ideals
rows: {@b Truth.} I want to uncover what other secrets the world may have in store. (Any)
{@b Survival.} The world is harsh, and I will ensure that the people I care about survive and prosper. (Neutral)
{@b Stewardship.} I want to ensure that this world continues for future generations. (Good)
{@b Duty.} I have been given a purpose, and I will see it to its end. (Lawful)
{@b Domination.} The wilds do not pity the weak. (Evil)
{@b Exploration.} I want to see what this world has to offer beyond my hometown. (Neutral)

entries.caption: Bonds
rows: Ahydratmin saved my life. I will do anything to repay such a debt.
Someone I love has gone missing, and I now have the knowledge to find where they went.
Everyone thinks I'm some great hero now, and I don't know how to live up to that.
I was shown the location of a great artifact, and I must secure it before a rival does.
A face keeps appearing to me in a vision; I must find out who they are.
In the wild zone, I saw a vision of the future. I must stop a great calamity before it comes to pass.

entries.caption: Flaws
rows: My manners weren't good before I went missing for a year.
I refuse to listen to mundane problems anymore.
I'm often absent-minded, seeing too much at once.
Loud, crowded spaces might as well be Khyber.
I'm overly direct about my quest to the point of rudeness.
I'm brash at the best of moments.

_.special: small trinket evocative of Sarlona's ancient past

items.name: Skills:
items.entry: Choose two from among {@skill Arcana}, {@skill History}, {@skill Medicine}, and {@skill Nature}

items.name: Languages:
items.entry: Two of your choice

items.name: Suggested 1st level bonus feat:
items.entry: {@feat Magic Initiate} or {@feat Wild Talent|TalvakrisGuidetoAdar}

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: A collection of research notes, a set of {@item common clothes|PHB}, a {@item quill|PHB}, a bottle of ink, a small trinket evocative of Sarlona's ancient past, and a belt {@item pouch|PHB} containing 10 {@item gold|PHB}.

entries.name: Research Specialties
entries: As a Keeper of the Word, you are a scholar of some renown and respect. Most keepers spend their entire life devoted to aspects of Sarlona's history, some of which are niche even by your order's standards. You can select a Research Specialty below or roll randomly, then add details to determine how you ended up studying this subject over any other.

entries.caption: Research Specialties
rows: Rhiavhaaran sea shanties
Interactions between different planes
Pre-Sundering Khaleshite culture
Psionics and their history on Sarlona
Wild zones
Borunan war poetry

entries.name: Feature: Preservation of Knowledge
entries: You have easy access to all of Adar's libraries and stores of knowledge, most of which are concentrated in the nation's monastery-fortresses. This can range from knowledge of Pre-Sundering Sarlona to spells that can be copied into spellbooks. Dangerous spells and objects may be restricted until you have proven yourself to the wider organization. Should you uncover or develop previously unknown arcane spells, powers, or items, you are expected to contribute to the organization's overall knowledge.

entries.name: Suggested Traits
entries: Keepers of the Word come in all shapes and sizes. Are you pursuing knowledge for your own sake? Are you learning the history of Sarlona to reclaim your people's ancient histories? Or are you secretly looking to take all of that power for yourself?

entries.caption: Personality Traits
rows: I write everything down. Everything.
Other cultures fascinate me, especially if they're still alive after all this time.
I always keep trinkets on me; never know when you might need a Dolurrhi husksteel coin!
If I see something new, I will ask questions until the sun goes down.
The quieter the space, the better I work.
Given enough time, I will end up inventing something, and it will be someone else's problem.

entries.caption: Ideals
rows: {@b Knowledge.} I want to learn everything I can, no matter the danger or cost. (Any)
{@b Power.} The ancient sorcerers broke their world. I want that power for myself. (Evil)
{@b Innovation.} The past is a good foundation; progress comes from making their notes better. (Chaotic)
{@b Generosity.} The more we know, the better people's lives become. (Good)
{@b Preservation.} So much was lost in the Sundering. We cannot lose more of our history. (Neutral)
{@b Traditions.} Sharing knowledge and keeping our structure ensures our order endures. (Lawful)

entries.caption: Bonds
rows: My teacher killed my classmates, and I want revenge.
My ancestors fled from the place I now try to study.
I want to rise in the ranks of the Keepers, someday becoming a teacher myself.
I was almost killed for something I wrote down; I want to find out why.
An ancient text appeared in my study, and I'm too curious to leave well enough alone.
A loved one died trying to uncover a piece of our past; I want to finish their research so they can rest.

entries.caption: Flaws
rows: I have a one-track mind on the best of days.
Hiding something from me is akin to betrayal.
I'm far too fond of cruel spells and stories.
Give me enough time and I will end up creating trouble.
I'd rather die than keep a secret from other Keepers.
Aloof is probably too kind a word for me.

_.special: set of trinkets from various Khyber demiplanes

items.name: Skills:
items.entry: {@skill Acrobatics}, {@skill Athletics}

items.name: Languages:
items.entry: {@language Infernal} and Dromite

items.name: Suggested 1st level bonus feat:
items.entry: Mobile ({@book Player's Handbook|PHB})

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: A hooded lantern and oil, a set of {@item traveler's clothes|PHB}, a sturdy bag, a set of trinkets from various Khyber demiplanes, and a {@item pouch|PHB} containing 20 {@item gold|PHB}.

entries.name: Belly of the Beast
entries: Familiarity with Khyber is a dangerous thing. The longer you spend within the Dragon Below, the more it changes you, both physically and mentally. You can select a Scar of the Realm Below or roll randomly, then add details to determine how you survived the encounter that scarred you for life.

entries.caption: Scars of the Realm Below
rows: A symbiont has attached to your arm; it's harmless — for now.
Your humming sounds more like buzzing.
Your shadow moves on its own, whispering secrets to everyone you pass by.
You lost a finger — or maybe it never existed in the first place.
Sickly green light pours from your eyes and mouth.
You always taste blood on your lips.

entries.name: Feature: Reliable Routes
entries: You have been into the Realm Below more times than you can count. You know of reliable routes between demiplanes, and you know the safest — though not safe — paths through. You can barter with other Khyber couriers for new paths after gaining their trust. As a Khyber courier, you may be asked to deliver letters or packages to far-flung locales in exchange for knowledge, or people may seek you out to pay for these services. At your DM's discretion, you may also have contacts who live within the Realm Below, though they will not always be friendly to you.

entries.name: Suggested Traits
entries: Not just anyone runs into Khyber on the regular. What made you take up this job? What has made you stay? Have you kept your morals and your sanity, or have you decided that those are for less... enlightened minds?

entries.caption: Personality Traits
rows: I perform bizarre rituals before doing anything—and I mean anything.
I'm overly polite to everyone I meet.
I'm jumpy on a good day; anyone would be in my line of work.
Favors are easier to carry than gold, and in my line of work, almost always more useful.
I will never turn down exploring somewhere bizarre, especially if it's a place I've never been before.
I routinely forget that mundane animals don't spit acid. Or fire. Or try to pull water from my skin.

entries.caption: Ideals
rows: {@b Service.} Through acid rain or radiant shine, the job must be done. (Any)
{@b Curiosity.} There are wonders amongst the dangers, and it calls to me. (Chaotic)
{@b Influence.} The more factions I know, the more people I can call on for favors. (Neutral)
{@b Corruption.} Khyber has claimed me, and I want it to claim others as well. (Evil)
{@b Communication.} Keeping my people connected is how we will continue to survive. (Good)
{@b Survival.} I will do anything to survive the Realm Below. (Neutral)

entries.caption: Bonds
rows: I have a message with no address and must find its recipient.
My best friend went missing in Khyber and I have been trying to find them—or what's left of them.
I fled into Khyber to escape Riedra; I promised that I would come back for someone I left behind.
I was entrusted with information that only one person may ever hear; I must find them.
I was forced to leave something behind in Khyber; I'm not getting it back unless I go back into that hell.
A mysterious patron offered me a deal in Khyber, and I do not know when they will come to collect.

entries.caption: Flaws
rows: I am intensely paranoid.
Displays of magic freak me out, especially if it looks gruesome.
I believe I am surrounded by Cultists of the Dragon Below.
I have an irrational hatred for all kinds of plants.
I'm convinced I'm being watched at all times.
I refuse to help people when I'm on a job; those screams might be a trap.

_.special: trinket from your time in Riedra

items.name: Skills:
items.entry: {@skill Survival}, {@skill Persuasion}

items.name: Languages:
items.entry: Riedran

items.name: Tool Proficiencies:
items.entry: One type of {@item artisan's tools}

items.name: Suggested 1st level bonus feat:
items.entry: {@feat Magic Initiate} or {@feat Resilient}

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: A trinket from your time in Riedra, a set of {@item common clothes|PHB}, a set of artisan's tools (your choice), a {@item knife|PHB}, and a belt {@item pouch|PHB} containing 5 {@item gold|PHB}.

entries.name: Why Did You Leave?
entries: For all the horrors that Riedra inflicts upon its people — the lack of privacy, intense social control, lack of choice and free will — there are many benefits. No one goes hungry. People are safe from war and disease. Everyone has a bed to sleep in and clean clothes to wear. For many in Riedra, this is enough — why wasn't it for you? You can select a Tipping Point below or roll randomly, then add details to determine your former place in Riedran society.

entries.caption: Tipping Points
rows: You learned who really rules Riedra, and you fled before anyone could find out.
You were part of a rebel group that was infiltrated by the Thousand Eyes. Everyone in your sect is now dead.
You developed sorcerous magic, and you know you wouldn't live long if someone found out.
Someone you loved vanished — and you know they will never return.
An Adaran saved your life, planting a seed of doubt into the motives of the Unity of Riedra.
You were a victim of Riedra's oppressive caste system, and you wanted to experience true freedom.

entries.name: Feature: Lingering Memories
entries: You are well-integrated into the cultural norms and practices of Riedra. You know how to act around Riedran guards and people in positions of authority, passing yourself off as a loyal member of society. If you are a changeling, people treat you with great respect. While other Sarlonans may trust you less, it is easier for you to blend into Riedran spaces, and you will be questioned far less than an outsider.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: As a Riedran refugee, you are starting from scratch. How has your time away from the Unity changed you? What do you miss? With every choice in the world now available, what do you even want to do?

entries.caption: Personality Traits
rows: I am anxious around people with more authority than me.
I'm not about to be told what to do ever again.
Out of habit, I still give prayers to the il-altas to protect me.
I cling to people who can give me a sense of purpose.
Injustice and oppression make me violently angry — emphasis on the violently.
I used to have status, and the lack of automatic respect has been jarring at best.

entries.caption: Ideals
rows: {@b Freedom.} I will never trade security for personal choice ever again. (Chaotic)
{@b Righteousness.} Free of Riedra, I've been given a chance to try and make my world better. (Good)
{@b Safety.} The most important thing is that I live long enough to enjoy my new home. (Neutral)
{@b Devastation.} Riedra deserves to burn to the ground, and I don't care who gets hurt in the process. (Evil)
{@b Revelry.} I have been given the chance to be myself, and I am living life to its fullest. (Good)
{@b Rebellion.} There are people still worth fighting for in Riedra. (Good)

entries.caption: Bonds
rows: A good friend of mine is still in Riedra, and I want to try and get them out.
I would do anything to protect my newfound refugee community.
I stole something important from an Inspired — and I know I'm hunted by the Thousand Eyes.
My birth parents fled Riedra long ago, and I want to find out if they made it out alive.
I need to pay back the Summit Guide who got me out.
The Inspired and the Thousand Eyes killed someone I knew well; revenge will not be enough for me.

entries.caption: Flaws
rows: I have no idea how to advocate for myself.
My illiteracy doesn't help me on the best of days.
I panic if I am given orders, even by a close friend.
I still think the people around me are dealing with demons.
The lack of structure fills me with choice paralysis.
I hold little sympathy for the people I left behind; they are little better than sheep to me.

items.name: Skills:
items.entry: {@skill Stealth}, {@skill Survival}

items.name: Tool Proficiencies:
items.entry: {@item Thieves' tools|PHB}

items.name: Languages:
items.entry: Sign language (mutually intelligible with thieves' cant) and one of your choice

items.name: Suggested 1st level bonus feat:
items.entry: {@feat Observant} or {@feat Skilled}

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: A set of {@item traveler's clothes|PHB}, a {@item climber's kit|PHB}, silk rope (50 feet), and a belt {@item pouch|PHB} containing 15 {@item gold|PHB}.

entries.name: Cell Specialty
entries: Every part of the Summit Road operates independently, and every cell is self-sufficient. Each group has scouts, trackers, smugglers, and magic users, and it's not uncommon for members to practice several professions at once. You can select a Specialty below or roll randomly, then add details to determine the other members of your cell.

entries.caption: Specialties
rows: Scout
Spy
Mage or psion
Smuggler
Cartographer
Thief

entries.name: Feature: Summit Cell
entries: As a member of the Summit Road, you have close contact with other members of your specific cell. Each cell has approximately five to eight guides, all of whom can operate independently but have a wide range of skills. Through your cell, you can easily send and receive messages throughout Adar. Your cell can also provide you with resources and magical services if need be, though you will be expected to return the favor.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: The life of a Summit Guide is a hard one at best. Do you do this out of a sense of duty? Is this the only way you could survive being exiled for a crime? How do you handle one of the most dangerous jobs in Adar — and how have you survived despite the costs?

entries.caption: Personality Traits
rows: I've been told a mule is nicer than me.
I try to be kind to the kids at least.
I always keep a dagger under my pillow... or just in my hand.
When it's safe, I love telling stories of my missions.
Paranoia and I are old friends.
The adrenaline rush of a mission never gets old.

entries.caption: Ideals
rows: {@b Escape.} All that matters is getting people out; better to die than be captured. (Any)
{@b Order.} If we don't stick to our plans, we're as good as dead in this line of work. (Lawful)
{@b Chaos.} The less predictable we are, the less likely we are to be caught. (Chaos)
{@b Refuge.} We strive to get people to safety. (Good)
{@b Knowledge.} The more we know, the better chance we have to survive. (Any)
{@b Cruelty.} Only the strong survive this path; cut out the weak parts of our organization. (Evil)

entries.caption: Bonds
rows: My cell is my family now; everyone else has rejected me.
I was saved by the Summit Road; I want to go back for someone else.
Someone was captured and only I can save them.
I took up this job for the money; once I get enough, I want to go home to my family.
My mentor was cursed on a mission, and I want to find the cure.
One of my cell members defected to the Thousand Eyes, and I have vowed to stop them.

entries.caption: Flaws
rows: I'd rather die than be captured.
Given the choice between saving someone and staying alive, I will always choose myself.
I'm too nice, something I know will get me killed.
I believe people in my life have been replaced with spies.
I trust absolutely no one, not even myself at times.
The ends always justify the means.

backgroundFluff.name: Chosen of the Mountainsoul
entries: At some point in your life, you chose to walk through Ahdryatmin, the great Lamannian wild zone at Adar's heart. Whether a trip made out of necessity or religious devotion, you did not come out the same. The ancient spirit within the wild zone blessed you, and with it, charged you with grand purpose. Your patron is enigmatic, certainly, but with their blessing comes wisdom—and perhaps other benefits as well.

backgroundFluff.name: Keeper of the Word
entries: You belong to the Keepers of the Word, an order of arcanists established by exiles from around Sarlona. Your order is dedicated to uncovering arcane secrets, and you have vowed to ensure nothing is lost again, even if that knowledge is dangerous in the wrong hands. You are a researcher, a historian, and a bit nosy; expect other Adarans to consider you all three.

backgroundFluff.name: Khyber Courier
entries: Running into Khyber is madness. Regularly running through Khyber is your specialty. The dromite city states have long trained runners to go through Khyber, delivering messages and packages over long distances. Long ago, this was one of many mail-carrying services that Sarlona had access to; these days, it is the only way to deliver something from Tashana to Adar in a day.

Your route might not have many destinations; in fact, it's probably safest that you only know of a few routes. Even so, if someone needs to get from one end of Sarlona to the other in a day, you just might know the way—so long as they don't mind running very, very fast.

backgroundFluff.name: Riedran Refugee
entries: You ran away. By some combination of miracle and desperation, you made it out of Riedra and into Adar. Your life has not been easy, and in fact has gotten harder in many ways. You barely know how to read and write. The concept of calendars and days of the week is still foreign. You dream your own dreams, and you have to navigate a world where you finally have to make your own choices. But for you, this still beats living under Riedra's thumb, because anything would be better than living in the shadow of the Unity.

backgroundFluff.name: Summit Guide
entries: You are one of Adar's finest, a guide who has been trusted to brave the paths in and out of the Land of Refuge. It's a dangerous life at best, and you have risked life and limb time and time again to bring refugees to safety. Your mind is hardened, and you know there are worse things than death if you are caught. But for every danger the Summit Road brings, it is worth it to see another Riedran find a way to freedom.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: Thanks to your compound, insectoid eyes, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in {@quickref Vision and Light||2||dim light} within 60 feet of you as if it were {@quickref Vision and Light||2||bright light}, and in darkness as if it were {@quickref Vision and Light||2||dim light}. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Essence Form
entries: You developed an affinity for a particular essence when you grew to full maturity. Choose an essence from the Essence Forms table. Your choice of essence cannot be changed, and determines the damage type for your other traits, as shown on the table.

entries.caption: Essence Form
rows: Glimmer
Lightning
Ice
Cold
Torch
Fire
Voice
Thunder

entries.name: Psychokinetic Ray
entries: You can channel psionic power to fire psychokinetic rays. As an action, you fire a number of rays equal to half your Proficiency Bonus (rounded up).

For each ray, you make a spell attack against an object or creature within 90 feet of you. On a hit you deal damage of the type associated with your Essence Form equal to {@dice 2d8} + your spellcasting ability modifier. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these rays (choose when you select this species).
You can use this action a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Damage Resistance
entries: You have Resistance to the damage type determined by your Essence Form.

entries.name: Insectoid Senses
entries: You have antennae that supplement your other senses. You have proficiency in the {@skill Perception} skill.

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: You are an Aberration, not a Humanoid.

entries.name: Size
entries: Typically the transformation process to become an Elan causes a person to grow a few inches taller than they once were. You are Medium or Small. You choose the size when you select this species.

entries.name: Aberrant Nature
entries: You do not need to sleep, and cannot be magically aged or put to sleep. Additionally, you can't dream, and are immune to magical effects that require you to dream.

When you take a long rest, you must spend six hours in suspended animation instead of sleeping. While in this state of suspended animation you are fully aware of your surroundings, but unable to move or take any actions.

Your suspended animation ends early if you take damage, or another creature within reach of you uses an action to physically rouse you from it. If you resume your suspended animation within 1 hour of it being interrupted, the time you already spent in it is not lost.

entries.name: Telepathic Bond
entries: You can speak telepathically to any creature you can see, provided the creature is within a number of feet of you equal to 10 times your level. You don't need to share a language with the creature for it to understand your telepathic utterances, but the creature must be able to understand at least one language. Additionally, as an action, you can allow a creature within range to telepathically respond to you for 1 hour. This ends if you use this action on another creature.

entries.name: Psychic Deflection
entries: As a reaction when you take damage, you can reduce it by {@dice 1d12} + your Proficiency Bonus. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: You are a Humanoid.

entries.name: Size
entries: Though eneko are generally on the taller end, they're not nearly as large as true ogres or giants. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your walking speed is 30 feet.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light. You discern colors in that darkness only as shades of gray.

entries.name: Eneko Magic
entries: You carry a spark of the magic practiced by your giant ancestors. You can cast longstrider with this trait. Starting at 3rd level, you can cast pass without trace with this trait. Starting at 5th level, you can also cast protection from energy with it. Once you cast any of these spells with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast these spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level.
Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells when you cast them with this trait (choose when you select this species).

entries.name: Eneko Resilience
entries: You have resistance to poison damage and advantage on saving throws against the poisoned condition.

entries.name: Powerful Build
entries: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity, the amount of weight you can push, drag, or lift, and the sizes of creatures you can grapple.

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: You are a Humanoid.

entries.name: Size
entries: Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your walking speed is 30 feet.

entries.name: Goliath Psionics
entries: You know the thaumaturgy cantrip. Starting at 3rd level, you can cast the thunderwave spell with this trait. Starting at 5th level, you can also cast the enhance ability spell with it. Once you cast thunderwave or enhance ability with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast either of those spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level.

Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells when you cast them with this trait (choose when you select this species). None of these spells require spell components when you cast them with this trait.

entries.name: Mountain Born
entries: You have resistance to cold damage. You also naturally acclimate to high altitudes, even if you've never been to one. This includes elevations above 20,000 feet.

entries.name: Stone Grip
entries: You count as one size larger when grappling and determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: You are a Humanoid.

entries.name: Size
entries: Your size is Medium, but you cannot squeeze through spaces that would fit a Small creature.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your walking speed is 30 feet.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Natural Athlete
entries: You are proficient in the Athletics skill, and do not suffer a penalty to your movement while grappling a Medium or smaller creature.

entries.name: Long-Limbed
entries: When you make a melee attack on your turn, your reach for it is 5 feet greater than normal.

entries.name: Powerful Build
entries: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

entries.name: Yield No Ground
entries: You have advantage on ability checks and saving throws made to avoid being grappled or moved against your will by other creatures, and you cannot be dragged or carried by a creature grappling you unless they are Huge or larger.

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: You are a Humanoid.

entries.name: Size
entries: Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: You are a Humanoid.

entries.name: Size
entries: You are Medium or Small. You choose the size when you select this species.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your walking speed is 30 feet.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light. You discern colors in that darkness only as shades of gray.

entries.name: Couatl Psionics
entries: You can cast the cure wounds and guiding bolt spells with this trait. Once you cast either of these spells with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast these spells using any spell slots you have.

Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you gain this species). Neither of these spells require spell components when you cast them with this trait.

entries.name: Poison Resilience
entries: You have advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the poisoned condition on yourself. You also have resistance to poison damage.

entries.name: Telepathic Link
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you can speak telepathically to any creature you can see, provided the creature is within a number of feet of you equal to 10 times your level. You don't need to share a language with the creature for it to understand your telepathic utterances, but the creature must be able to understand at least one language. A creature without telepathy can receive and respond to telepathic messages but can't initiate or terminate a telepathic conversation. You can have a telepathic conversation with only one creature at a time.

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: You are a Humanoid.

entries.name: Size
entries: You are Medium or Small. You choose the size when you select this species.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your walking speed is 30 feet.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light. You discern colors in that darkness only as shades of gray.

entries.name: Oblivion Resistance
entries: You have resistance to force and necrotic damage.

entries.name: Omen of the End
entries: Other creatures within 10 feet of you have disadvantage on death saving throws. While you are conscious, creatures of your choice you can see are immune to this trait.

entries.name: Aura of Unmaking
entries: Starting at 3rd level, as a bonus action you can activate your aura, an emanation of annihilation and existential dread, that lasts for 1 minute or until you end it with another bonus action. While your aura is active, at the end of each of your turns, each creature within 10 feet of you takes force damage equal to your proficiency bonus and must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier) or become frightened of you until the end of your next turn.
Once you activate your aura, you can't activate it again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: You are a Humanoid.

entries.name: Size
entries: If you replace a species with this lineage, use your species to determine your size. If you choose this lineage at character creation, you are Medium or Small. You choose the size when you select this lineage.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

entries.name: Ancestral Legacy
entries: If you replace a species with this lineage, you can keep the following elements of that race: any skill proficiencies you gained from it and any climbing, flying, or swimming speed you gained from it.
If you don't keep any of those elements or you choose this lineage at character creation, you gain proficiency in two skills of your choice.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Chaotic Resistance
entries: You have a changing resistance to damage. When you finish a long rest, roll a d6 and consult the Chaotic Resistance table below for the damage type:

entries.caption: Chaotic Resistance
rows: Acid
Cold
Fire
Force
Lightning
Thunder

entries.name: Planetouched Nature
entries: When you choose this lineage, choose the nature of your planar tie to Kythri: Celestial, Elemental, or Fiendish. Your choice grants you one of the traits below:

Celestial. When you reach 3rd level, you can unleash the celestial energy within yourself as a bonus action, transforming yourself for 1 minute or until you end the transformation with another bonus action. When you transform, two luminous, spectral wings of chaotic energy sprout from your back. Until your transformation ends, you have a flying speed equal to your walking speed, and once on each of your turns, you can deal extra damage to one target when you deal damage to it with an attack or spell. The type of damage is the same as your current resistance granted by your Chaotic Resistance trait, and the amount of extra damage equals your proficiency bonus. Once you transform this way, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

Elemental. When you choose this lineage, choose one of the elements on the Chaotic Resistance table. You always have resistance to this type of damage, and this resistance is separate and in addition to the one granted by your Chaotic Resistance trait. If you roll this resistance on the table when you finish a long rest, reroll on the table until you gain a different resistance for your Chaotic Resistance trait.

Fiendish. You know the prestidigitation cantrip. Starting at 3rd level, you can cast the chaos bolt spell with this trait, without requiring a material component. Starting at 5th level, you can also cast the blink spell with this trait. Once you cast chaos bolt or blink with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast either of those spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level.
Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells when you cast them with this trait (choose when you select this lineage).

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: You are a Humanoid.

entries.name: Size
entries: If you replace a species with this lineage, use your species to determine your size. If you choose this lineage at character creation, you are Medium or Small. You choose the size when you select this lineage.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

entries.name: Ancestral Legacy
entries: If you replace a species with this lineage, you can keep the following elements of that race: any skill proficiencies you gained from it and any climbing, flying, or swimming speed you gained from it.
If you don't keep any of those elements or you choose this lineage at character creation, you gain proficiency in two skills of your choice.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Natural Resilience
entries: You are naturally acclimated to extreme heat, extreme cold, and high altitudes, including elevations over 20,000 feet. Additionally, you have resistance to poison damage and you have advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the poisoned condition on yourself.

entries.name: Planetouched Nature
entries: When you choose this lineage, choose the nature of your planar tie to Lamannia: Celestial, Elemental, or Fiendish. Your choice grants you one of the traits below:

Celestial. You can cast the animal friendship and goodberry spells with this trait. Once you cast either of these spells with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast these spells using any spell slots you have.
Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells when you cast them with this trait (choose when you select this race).

Elemental. You can hold your breath indefinitely while you're not incapacitated, and you can move across difficult terrain without expending extra movement if you are using your walking speed on the ground or a floor. Additionally, you can breathe air and water, and you gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed.

Fiendish. You know the primal savagery cantrip. Starting at 3rd level, you can cast the ensnaring strike spell with this trait. Starting at 5th level, you can also cast the spike growth spell with this trait. Once you cast ensnaring strike or spike growth with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast either of these spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level.
Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells when you cast them with this trait (choose when you select this lineage).

entries.name: Fiendish Wings
entries: You have vestigial wings. As a result, when you fall at least 10 feet, you can use your Reaction to safely glide downward, taking no damage from the fall. Additionally, when you make a Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check to jump, you can use your reaction to flap your wings and gain advantage on the ability check.

When you reach 5th level, you can channel your inner fiend to strengthen your wings, but must limit your reliance on them to avoid corruption. As a bonus action, your vestigial wings grow significantly in size for 10 minutes, until you are {@condition incapacitated} or you allow your wings to return to their natural vestigial state as a bonus action. While your wings are grown, you have a fly speed equal to your speed. Once you use this trait to grow your vestigial wings, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Forked Tongue
entries: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Deception} skill.

A cunning winged fiend, such as a horned devil or erinyes, is held by the oni of your bloodline. Lies and deceit come to you as easily as you breathe, and you can manifest fiendish wings to soar through the air - but only for short periods, lest you draw too hard on the evil held in your soul. Oni from Clan Naksha are lean, confident, and often smaller than their kin from other bloodlines.

entries.name: Master of Chains
entries: You can cast the {@spell ensnaring strike} and {@spell snare|XGE} spells with this trait. Once you cast either of these spells with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast these spells using any spell slots you have.

Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you gain this species). Neither of these spells require spell components when you cast them with this trait.

entries.name: Unbound
entries: You have advantage on saving throws made to avoid or end the {@condition paralyzed} condition on yourself.

In a turn of cosmic irony, your bloodline jails a chain devil—one of the Legion of Tyranny's diabolic captains and torturers. The fiend's innate powers allow you to manipulate ropes and chains, and grant familiarity with locks and restraints. Clan Morlu are hornless with ochrered skin, and often wield weapons such as whips and flails in battle.

entries.name: Arcane Intuition
entries: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Arcana} skill.

entries.name: Master of Lore
entries: You can cast the {@spell detect magic} and {@spell identify} spells with this trait without requiring components. Once you cast either of these spells with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast these spells as rituals or you may cast them using any spell slots you have.
Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you gain this species). None of these spells require spell components when you cast them with this trait.

Clan Shallak bear the burden of an imprisoned arcanaloth mercenary—one of the Legion of Tyranny's dangerous war mages. The fiend grants you an innate sense for the workings of magic, and an understanding of the workings of spells. Many oni of Clan Shallak once trained as battlesages and spellsmiths within the Cauldron. Such oni often have sharp fangs and bestial eyes as a mark of their heritage.

entries.name: Bone Plating
entries: Sections of your skin are covered in bony plates. While you aren't wearing armor, your base Armor Class is 13 + your Dexterity modifier.

entries.name: Fiendish Resistance
entries: Your devil heritage gives you resistance to poison damage and advantage on saving throws made to avoid or end the {@condition poisoned} condition on yourself.

entries.name: Natural Weapons
entries: Your hands end in sharp talons that you can use to make unarmed strikes. When you hit with them, the attack deals slashing damage equal to {@dice 1d6} + your Strength modifier, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

A powerful bone devil commander was captured and bound to your bloodline. The oni of Clan Granuk are pale, with bony plates over sections of their body and razorsharp claws. Many once served on the frontlines of Gul Dol as masters of unarmed combat.

raceFluff.name: Dromite
entries: You are a dromite, a member of a vast underground society of hive cities and psionic prowess. You have spent nearly your entire life underground, venturing to the surface to barter for goods or explore lands beyond rock and stone. For some, this is a great honor, as you may have served as a guide or mail carrier. For others, this has led only to tragedy at the hands of the Unity of Riedra.

raceFluff.name: Elan
entries: Ever since you awoke, you were told that you were chosen for a purpose. A great evil spirit had been imprisoned with you, and you were meant to live a life so virtuous that the spirit within you would eventually be redeemed. It wasn't entirely a lie; not necessarily.

However, eventually, you left the Unity of Riedra behind. Only you can say why. Maybe you grew disillusioned with the supposed perfect world you were trying to protect. Perhaps you learned the truth, that you used to be someone else and those memories were wiped away to turn you into a living prison. All you know is that you do not want to go back—and you have a long, long life ahead of you, as elan can live upwards of several thousand years—and their maximum lifespan is currently unknown.

raceFluff.name: Eneko
entries: Originally appeared in Linvakri's Guide to Syrkarn.

Eneko are Sarlona's half-elves, part ogre, part goliath, and one whole person. Your culture has developed on the wide-open plains of Syrkarn where you learned to value freedom and self-expression. Your heart is strong, your smile is wide, and neither Riedran patrols nor Adaran mountains will stop you from achieving your goals, whether that be researching your people's ancient past or walking along the Summit Road.

raceFluff.name: Goliath (Psionic)
entries: It is unknown exactly when the goliaths of Sarlona began practicing psionics. Some claim that their distant ancestors in Xen'drik once practiced psionics to their great devastation. Others believe the scars of their ancestors' wars with 'dreams and nightmares' scarred their people's souls to the point where their distant descendants still bear the burden of that calamity. Regardless of the origins, you were born with a bit more psionic potential than your peers, something you use to great advantage in the mountains of Adar.

raceFluff.name: Ogre
entries: Originally Written by Jamie Bernstein.

Long ago, the ogres of Sarlona lived in Borunan, fighting an eternal war alongside the angels of Shavarath. The Inspired thoroughly crushed this ancient tradition, forcing the once-proud ogres into a life of manual labor, servitude, and dehumanization. Others fled into Syrkarn or Adar, carrying the shame of abandoning their ancient allies. The future of your people is uncertain. Do you try and reclaim your birthright? Do you use the powers of old for your own designs?

raceFluff.name: Oni
entries: Originally Written by Jamie Bernstein.

The ogres of Borunan once bound many types of fiends to their bloodlines, creating the first oni. Once upon a time, these oni lines were once formed into great clans, and you carry a spark of that ancient legacy. Borunan oni stand between 6–8 feet tall depending on their fiendish origin and weigh between 200–400 pounds. Oni reach adulthood at 20 and live up to 150 years.

raceFluff.name: Shulassakar
entries: Originally appeared in Sarhain's Guide to the Silver Flame.

The couatl-blessed are some of the rarest people in Adar, guarding ancient ruins and combating the Endseekers that stalk the mountainsides. You are a bloodsworn, sporting colorful feathers that can be hidden behind hair and clothing, but otherwise look human enough to blend into the nation's many temple-keeps.

raceFluff.name: Unmade
entries: You do not have the luxury of forgetting that the Unmaker rages beneath the weight of Korrandar. You are a sakah, a tiefling who has gained their fiendish influence from an Overlord, and the influence of Ran Iishiv is written on your disintegrating horns and shattered ceramic skin. Though mortal and capable of choosing your own destiny, many consider you a sign of ill omen at best.

raceFluff.name: Kythric Planetouched
entries: Chaos runs through your veins, quite literally. The Churning Chaos has imbued you with elemental power, shifting and ever-changing. If you are an aasimar, sporting a celestial connection to the Churning Chaos, you may have a glowing elemental halo and technicolor wings. If you are a genasi, your elemental connection shows in lava cracking your skin and hair always blowing in the wind. If you are a tiefling with a spark of chaotic fiendish power, you may sport branching horns, cloven feet, and a wide array of scales and spines and sulfuric breaths.

raceFluff.name: Lamannian Planetouched
entries: Unbridled and unbound nature has left its mark upon you. Celestial animals guide aasimar, known for befriending those they meet along the way with hawk-like eyes and intricate leaf-like hair. Lamannian genasi look like the nature spirits and elementals of the plane, skin textured like bark and hair like great bushes or flowers. While there are few fiends within Lamannia, tieflings sport great branching horns, sharp teeth, and long claws.
